info.title: Brilliant Directories API
info.description:
Universal API for Brilliant Directories websites. Manage members, content, leads, email templates, categories, and more. Works with any BD-powered site — just provide your site URL and API key.

## Authentication
All requests require an X-Api-Key header. Keys are created in BD Admin > Developer Hub > Generate API Key (see https://support.brilliantdirectories.com/support/solutions/articles/12000088768).

## Rate Limiting
Default limit: **100 requests per 60 seconds per API key.**

Customers can request a higher limit from Brilliant Directories support — any value between **100 and 1,000 requests per minute.** This is set server-side by BD on request; it is NOT a self-service setting in the BD admin. If you expect heavy API usage, contact BD support before bulk operations.

When the limit is exceeded, the API returns **HTTP 429 Too Many Requests**. Clients and AI agents should:
- For bulk operations (imports, batch updates, mass exports), pace requests below the configured limit
- On receiving 429, back off and retry after at least 60 seconds
- For known-large jobs, either ask BD support to raise the limit first OR process in batches with delays

The /api/v2/token/verify endpoint can be called before bulk jobs to confirm credentials work.

## Pagination
List endpoints use cursor-based pagination via limit + page params. Default page size is 25, max 100.

## Error Format
All errors: { "status": "error", "message": "..." } with standard HTTP codes (400 bad request, 401 unauthorized, 404 not found, 429 rate limited, 500 server error).
info.x-api-version-note: The info.version field represents BD's REST API version (currently v2 — /api/v2/* endpoints), NOT this MCP wrapper's release version.
info.x-mcp-wrapper-version: See https://www.npmjs.com/package/brilliant-directories-mcp for the current MCP wrapper version (npm package).

bd_site_url.description: The full URL of your BD-powered website (e.g. https://yoursite.com)

apiKey.name: X-Api-Key
apiKey.description: Your BD API key. Generate in BD Admin > Developer Hub > Generate API Key.

page.name: page
page.description: Cursor token for pagination (use next_page value from previous response)

property.name: property
property.description: Field name to filter by (use property[] for multiple)

property_value.name: property_value
property_value.description: Value to filter by (use property_value[] for multiple)

property_operator.name: property_operator
property_operator.description: Filter operator: =, LIKE, >, <, >=, <= (use property_operator[] for multiple)

review_status.description:
Review status — authoritative values from BD admin:
  0 = Pending            (newly submitted, awaiting moderation)
  2 = Accepted           (approved and visible on the member profile)
  3 = Declined           (rejected by admin — not publicly visible)
  4 = Waiting for Admin  (member has pre-accepted, admin sign-off still required)
Value 1 is NOT valid. Normal moderation flow: 0 → 2 (accepted) or 0 → 3 (declined). Use 4 when a member has accepted but admin review is still pending.

master_id.description: 0 for top-level, parent item ID for sub-items

get.description:
Verify that your API key is valid and check rate limit status.

**Use when:** at the start of any session or batch job, to confirm the API key is valid and the site is reachable BEFORE burning rate limit on real calls. Also useful for surfacing a clear "bad credentials" error to the user early.

**Parameter interactions:**
- Call at the start of a session to confirm the API key is valid BEFORE issuing real calls — saves rate-limit budget on key-config errors

**Returns:** `{ status: "success"|"error", message: ... }` — BD's standard response envelope.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

Returns available fields for user records with labels and required flags. Use this to discover custom fields.

**Use when:** building dynamic forms or importers — you need to discover which fields exist on the User record on THIS specific site (custom fields vary per BD site config). Also useful for validating import-CSV headers before running a batch.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: [{...record}] }` — the `message` array contains 1 record when found. Empty or HTTP 404 when not found.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

Get a paginated list of all members. Supports filtering by any user field and sorting.

**Use when:** enumerating members for reports, CSV exports, bulk status updates, analytics, or pagination through the full member base. Also used for lookups by field — pass `property=email` + `property_value=<email>` to find a single user by email. For keyword/text search use `searchUsers`; for a single user by known `user_id` use `getUser`.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` token from the previous response's `next_page`. Do not assume integer offsets.

**Filter/sort:** `property` + `property_value` + `property_operator` (`=`, `LIKE`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`) to filter; `order_column` + `order_type` (`ASC`/`DESC`) to sort. Array syntax `property[]=...&property_value[]=...&property_operator[]=...&property_logic[]=AND|OR` is supported for multi-condition filters.

**Enums:** `property_operator`: `=`, `LIKE`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`; `order_type`: `ASC`, `DESC`.

**Filter-property rule — use ACTUAL field names:** `property` must reference a real column on `users_data` or a valid custom user field. If you don't know what's filterable, call `getUserFields` first — it returns the authoritative list for this site (includes custom fields). BD returns misleading errors like `"user not found"` when `property` names a nonexistent field — that is a BAD FILTER, not a 404 on the endpoint. Do not invent properties like `user_group` (not a real column).

**Filtering by TOP CATEGORY (profession):** the filter column is `profession_id` (integer), not a category name string. If the caller gives you a category name, chain: (1) `listTopCategories` → find the row whose `name` matches; (2) grab its `profession_id`; (3) call `listUsers` with `property=profession_id&property_value=<id>`. Same principle for any taxonomy filter — resolve names to IDs first via `listSubCategories`, `listMembershipPlans`, etc. For sub-category filtering on users, the authoritative approach is `listMemberSubCategoryLinks` filtered by `service_id` → collect `user_id`s → fetch those users. (There is also a `service` CSV column on user records but exact-match filtering on it requires the complete CSV value and LIKE syntax support is not guaranteed — prefer the link-table route.)

**Filtering by `users_meta` (custom/meta fields):** multi-value meta filtering IS supported via the array syntax — e.g. `property[]=<meta_key>&property_value[]=foo&property[]=<meta_key>&property_value[]=bar&property_operator[]=OR&property_logic[]==`. Use this for OR-across-meta-values lookups (e.g. members whose custom field equals any of N options).

**Payment-method field in response:** every user record includes `card_info`. When no card is on file it is the literal boolean `false` (BD's convention — same as `tags: false`, `transactions: false`, etc.). When a card IS stored, it's an object with fields like `last4`, `brand`, `name`. Check `card_info && card_info.last4` (truthy-guard) — NOT `card_info.last4` directly, since `false.last4` throws. This is the authoritative signal for "does this member have a valid payment method on file" — do not infer from `subscription_id` alone. BD clears `card_info` back to `false` when the member removes their card; it does not go stale silently.

**See also:** `getUser` (single record by ID), `searchUsers` (keyword search), `getUserFields` (list filterable fields).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", total, current_page, total_pages, next_page, prev_page, message: [...records] }`. Each record is the full resource object.

**Profile URL:** every user record has a `filename` field. To get the full public profile URL, concatenate: `<site-domain>/<user.filename>`. The `filename` is the complete relative path (e.g., `united-states/monterey-park/doctor/harrison-hasanuddin-d-o`) — DO NOT prepend `/business/`, `/profile/`, `/member/`, or any other segment. BD's router resolves `filename` verbatim.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

Fetch a single user record. Read-only.

**Use when:** you already have the `user_id` (from `listUsers`, `searchUsers`, a prior create, or a webhook payload) and need the full member record. Cheaper than `listUsers` + filter. For lookups by email or other field, use `listUsers` with `property`/`property_value`.

**Required:** `user_id`.

**See also:** `listUsers` (enumerate many), `searchUsers` (keyword search).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: [{...record}] }` — the `message` array contains 1 record when found. Empty or HTTP 404 when not found.

**Payment method on file:** the response includes a `card_info` field. When no card is stored it is the literal boolean `false` (BD convention). When a card IS stored, it's an object with fields like `last4`, `brand`, `name`. Use `card_info && card_info.last4` to safely check — `card_info.last4` would throw on the `false` case. This is the authoritative signal for "does this member have a payment method" — don't infer from `subscription_id` alone. BD clears `card_info` back to `false` when the member removes their card; it does not go stale silently.

**Profile URL:** every user record has a `filename` field. To get the full public profile URL, concatenate: `<site-domain>/<user.filename>`. The `filename` is the complete relative path (e.g., `united-states/monterey-park/doctor/harrison-hasanuddin-d-o`) — DO NOT prepend `/business/`, `/profile/`, `/member/`, or any other segment. BD's router resolves `filename` verbatim. **Note:** `filename` is regenerated by BD when member inputs that influence the slug change (category, city, etc.). The value you see NOW is current-as-of-this-read. If you call `updateUser` afterward, re-fetch before using the filename in URL-referencing content (blog posts, emails, redirects).

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

active.description:
The status of the user. BD does NOT validate this field — integers outside the documented set are stored as-is (observed: `99`). Stick to documented values.

Documented values:
  1 = Not Active - Account Still Requires Activation
  2 = Active - Account is Live
  3 = Canceled - Sets User as Canceled Status
  4 = On Hold - Account Requires Moderation
  5 = Past Due - Sets User as Past Due Status
  6 = Incomplete - Paid signup hit an issue BD didn't account for; member was created but stays Incomplete instead of Active (without having paid). Rare edge state.

Reading: the top-level `status` response field ("Active", "Not Active", "Incomplete", etc.) is a computed label. When `active` is an unknown value, `status` is OMITTED from the response — do not treat `status` as always-present.

listing_type.description: Listing type classification. **BD does NOT validate this field — any string is stored verbatim.** Canonical values (exact case): `Individual` or `Company`. On `createUser`, ALWAYS include this field explicitly — never omit it and never send off-canonical values (`individual`, `INDIVIDUAL`, `Business`, etc. will store as-is and break downstream logic that checks `listing_type === "Individual"`). Default to `Company`, with 3 override paths (in priority order): (1) the user explicitly says `Individual`, (2) a structured input like a CSV row / API payload specifies `listing_type` per-record — use that value but normalize case client-side, (3) the source description clearly describes a person rather than a business/organization. BD's server-side default is `Individual` if omitted — do NOT rely on that.

verified.description:
If YES, a verified icon badge will display on the user's listing.

Valid values:
  1 = Yes
  0 = No

signup_date.description: Date the user signed up. Format: `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` (14-digit timestamp, no separators — e.g. `20260419143022` for 2026-04-19 14:30:22). Auto-filled by BD on create — omit unless backfilling a legacy signup date during import/migration.

profession_id.description: Assign this user to a top level category. Input the ID number of the top level category.

services.description: Assign this user to a Sub level categories. Input a comma seperated list of the sub levels.

address2.description: The user's unit or suite number.

auto_geocode.description:
OPTIONAL: Allow Google to geocode users. Requires "Pretty URLs with Google Maps". Search BD support to learn more.

Valid values:
  1 = Yes
  0 = No

state_ln.description: OPTIONAL: Enter the full name of the state / province for this user.

country_ln.description: OPTIONAL: Enter the full name of the country for this user.

lat.description: OPTIONAL: Enter latitude coordinates for the location of this user.

lon.description: OPTIONAL: Enter longitude coordinates for the location of this user.

nationwide.description:
If YES, the user's listing will be found in all geographical location searches.

Valid values:
  1 = Yes
  0 = No

search_description.description: The short description about this user. Displays under your user's name on search result pages. 170 Character Limit. Pass raw HTML — do NOT wrap in `<![CDATA[...]]>`, do NOT escape as `<` / `>`. BD stores the field value verbatim; any wrapper/escape gets saved as literal text.

about_me.description: The long description about this user. Pass raw HTML — do NOT wrap in `<![CDATA[...]]>`, do NOT escape as `<` / `>`. BD stores the field value verbatim; any wrapper/escape gets saved as literal text.

quote.description: OPTIONAL: Enter the user's personal quote, motto or slogan.

experience.description: OPTIONAL: Enter the year that the user's company was established. Example: 1982

affiliation.description: OPTIONAL: Enter the accepted forms of payment this user accepts.

awards.description: OPTIONAL: Enter honors, awards or accolades this user has received.

rep_matters.description: OPTIONAL: Enter the hours of operation for the member. EG: Monday - Friday, 9am - 5pm

position.description: OPTIONAL: Enter the user's position, title or role at their company. Example: Account Executive

website.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's website. Must begin with https://

booking_link.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's booking page. Must begin with https://

blog.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's blog. Must begin with https://

facebook.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's Facebook account. Must begin with https://

twitter.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's Twitter account. Must begin with https://

linkedin.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's Linkedin account. Must begin with https://

youtube.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's YouTube account. Must begin with https://

instagram.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's Instagram account. Must begin with https://

pinterest.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's Pinterest account. Must begin with https://

tiktok.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's Tiktok account. Must begin with https://

snapchat.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's Snapchat account. Must begin with https://

whatsapp.description: Enter the FULL URL of the user's Whatsapp account. Must begin with https://

profile_photo.description: OPTIONAL: Enter the FULL URL of the image filename for the user's profile photo. Image URL must already exists somewhere online in order for this field to work properly. URL should begin with http://

logo.description: OPTIONAL: Enter the FULL URL of the image filename for the user's profile logo. Image URL must already exists somewhere online in order for this field to work properly. URL should begin with http://

cover_photo.description: OPTIONAL: Enter the FULL URL of the image filename for the user's cover photo. Image URL must already exists somewhere online in order for this field to work properly. URL should begin with http://

auto_image_import.description:
If YES, system will import user images and save them to your website. Processing may take several minutes after import.

Valid values:
  1 = Yes
  0 = No

profession_name.description: Alternative to `profession_id` — pass the top-level category NAME as a string. BD looks it up in `list_professions`. On `createUser`, unknown names are auto-created. On `updateUser`, unknown names are silently skipped unless `create_new_categories=1` is also passed.

send_email_notifications.description: Set to `1` to trigger the welcome email on member creation (based on the membership plan's configured email). Default: off — API creates are silent because they typically aren't self-signups. Only set this when you WANT the member notified.

last_login.description: Date/time of the user's last login. Format: `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` (14-digit timestamp, no separators). Auto-maintained by BD on each login — omit unless backfilling historical login data during import/migration.

filename.description: Override BD's auto-generated URL slug (e.g. `united-states/los-angeles/doctor/jane-smith`). If omitted, BD derives it from city/category/name. Usually OMIT — manual values get regenerated by BD on future updates that change URL-influencing fields, which can silently overwrite your override.

post.description:
Create a new user record. Writes live data.

**Use when:** adding a member from outside the BD signup form — CSV imports, web-scraped business listings, Zapier/automation integrations, admin-created test accounts. By default no welcome email fires (set `send_email_notifications=1` to trigger one). Categories and sub-categories are auto-created by name — no need to `createTopCategory` first.

**Required:** `email`, `password`, `subscription_id`.

**Enums:** `active`: `1`=Not Active, `2`=Active, `3`=Canceled, `4`=On Hold, `5`=Past Due, `6`=Incomplete; `listing_type`: `Individual`=Individual Person, `Company`=Company / Organization; `verified`: `1`=Yes, `0`=No; `nationwide`: `1`=Yes, `0`=No.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `email`, `password`, `subscription_id` are all required together — `subscription_id` must be an existing membership plan (discover via `listMembershipPlans`)
- `profession_id` + `services` — top-level category and comma-separated sub-category IDs
- `auto_geocode=1` requires the "Pretty URLs with Google Maps" site feature enabled
- `auto_image_import=1` — **fetches external image URLs and saves them to BD site storage.** Applies to the `profile_photo`, `logo`, and `cover_photo` fields when populated with external URLs (e.g. URLs from a web-scrape). Without this flag, BD stores the external URL as-is; images may break if the source host goes down. **Recommended default when supplying image URLs from any external source** — only omit if the user explicitly asks to keep the external reference without local copy. Supports standard raster formats (JPG/PNG/GIF/WebP) AND vector SVG — URL-based source matters more than extension. Processing delay: several minutes after create.

**See also:** `updateUser` (modify existing).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for bulk operations. Changes are immediately visible on the live site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` — includes the server-assigned ID (e.g., `user_id`, `post_id`). Use this ID for follow-up operations.

**Profile URL:** every user record has a `filename` field. To get the full public profile URL, concatenate: `<site-domain>/<user.filename>`. The `filename` is the complete relative path (e.g., `united-states/monterey-park/doctor/harrison-hasanuddin-d-o`) — DO NOT prepend `/business/`, `/profile/`, `/member/`, or any other segment. BD's router resolves `filename` verbatim.

**Operational rules (from BD support article 12000091105):**
- `send_email_notifications=1` — set this to trigger the welcome email (based on the membership plan's email settings). Without this flag, no email is sent — API creates are silent by default (since they typically aren't self-signups from the public site).
- **Email uniqueness — depends on the site setting `allow_duplicate_member_emails`:**
  - Setting OFF (default for most sites): BD REJECTS duplicate `email` on create — you'll get a validation error.
  - Setting ON: duplicate `email` values are ACCEPTED and stored. Identical `email` + `password` pairs are also accepted (no extra uniqueness check on the pair).
  - **The setting is not exposed via API**, so agents cannot pre-determine which behavior this site uses. **Recommended safe pattern:** call `listUsers` with `property=email&property_value=<email>&property_operator==` first and check for existing records before create. If a match exists, reuse that `user_id` (confirm with the user for imports) or call `updateUser` instead. This is idempotent — retries/partial-failure recovery don't create duplicates. (Alternative: attempt the create and handle any rejection as 'duplicate, fall back'; less safe because on a site with duplicates allowed, BOTH inserts succeed with different user_ids and the same email — live-verified 2026-04-19. A retry loop without pre-check creates orphan duplicates.)
- **`token`** (if supplied): must be exactly 32 alphanumeric characters AND unique across all members.
- **URL fields** (`website`, `booking_link`, `blog`, `facebook`, `twitter`, `linkedin`, `instagram`, etc.) are validated on write; invalid formats are silently skipped (not rejected). Must start with `http://` or `https://`.
- **Prerequisites:** `subscription_id` MUST reference an existing membership plan (from `listMembershipPlans` / `createMembershipPlan`). Categories (`profession_id` / `profession_name` / `services`) are AUTO-CREATED on `createUser` if the names don't exist (see Category handling below) — they do NOT need to exist beforehand.



**Category handling — IDs OR names, with sub-sub support (source: user.php:1840-1940, verified live 2026-04-18):**

*Top-level category (profession) — pick ONE:*
- `profession_id` (numeric) — must reference an existing row in `list_professions`
- `profession_name` (string) — alternative by name

*`services` parameter (sub-categories) supports 4 formats:*
| Format | Example | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| Single by ID or name | `services="1823"` or `services="PVC repair"` | One sub-category |
| Multiple | `services="PVC repair,Water heater"` or `services="1823,1824"` | Multiple subs |
| Sub + sub-sub via `=>` | `services="Honda=>2022"` | Creates sub "Honda" and sub-sub "2022" under Honda |
| Mixed subs + sub-subs | `services="Honda=>2022,Honda=>2023,Toyota"` | Combine in one call |

*Critical rules:*
- **`profession_id` (or `profession_name`) is REQUIRED when passing `services`** — without it, service relationships fail silently (`user.php:1876`)
- **Do NOT mix IDs and names** within a single `services` call — pick one format
- **Right side of `=>` is NAME-ONLY** — no ID lookup; sub-sub must be passed by name
- **Whitespace around commas is trimmed** — `"a, b, c"` works the same as `"a,b,c"`

*Auto-create on create: ALWAYS ON.* `createUser` has `create_new_categories=true` hardcoded in `createActions()`. Unknown category/service/sub-sub names are auto-created automatically — no flag needed.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

put.description:
Update any user field. Supports special actions: member_tag_action + member_tags, credit_action + credit_amount, images_action, services, auto_geocode.

**Use when:** changing any field on an existing member — status changes (e.g., `active=3` to cancel instead of delete), tag edits, credit adjustments, category reassignment, profile image swaps. Prefer this over `deleteUser` for "remove from public view" since it's reversible. Note: changing `profession_id` wipes existing sub-category relations — re-send `services` in the same call to preserve them.

**Required:** `user_id`.

**Enums:** `active`: `1`=Not Active, `2`=Active, `3`=Canceled, `4`=On Hold, `5`=Past Due, `6`=Incomplete; `listing_type`: `Individual`=Individual Person, `Company`=Company / Organization; `verified`: `1`=Yes, `0`=No; `nationwide`: `1`=Yes, `0`=No.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `member_tag_action=1` + `member_tags` — apply tag changes (comma-separated tag IDs from `listTags`)
- `credit_action` (`add`/`deduct`/`override`) + `credit_amount` — adjust member credit balance
- `images_action` — remove stored images: `remove_all`, `remove_cover_image`, `remove_logo_image`, `remove_profile_image`
- `services` format: `category=>service1,service2` (category ID, then comma-separated service IDs)
- `auto_geocode=1` requires the "Pretty URLs with Google - `auto_image_import=1` — **fetches external image URLs and saves them to BD site storage.** Applies to the `profile_photo`, `logo`, and `cover_photo` fields when populated with external URLs (e.g. URLs from a web-scrape). Without this flag, BD stores the external URL as-is; images may break if the source host goes down. **Recommended default when supplying image URLs from any external source** — only omit if the user explicitly asks to keep the external reference without local copy. Supports standard raster formats (JPG/PNG/GIF/WebP) AND vector SVG — URL-based source matters more than extension. Processing delay: several minutes after create.
Maps" site feature enabled

**See also:** `createUser` (add new), `deleteUser` (remove permanently).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...updatedRecord} }` — the full updated record after changes applied.

**Profile URL:** every user record has a `filename` field. To get the full public profile URL, concatenate: `<site-domain>/<user.filename>`. The `filename` is the complete relative path (e.g., `united-states/monterey-park/doctor/harrison-hasanuddin-d-o`) — DO NOT prepend `/business/`, `/profile/`, `/member/`, or any other segment. BD's router resolves `filename` verbatim.

**Filename regenerates on update:** BD may rewrite the `filename` slug when this update changes category, city, or other URL-forming inputs (e.g., `/us/city/slug` → `/us/city/category/slug` after a category change). This is expected BD behavior, not a bug. Do NOT cache a filename from before this update — if you're embedding the profile URL in other content (blog posts, emails, redirects, another member's bio), re-fetch via `getUser` AFTER the update completes. Better yet, publish URL-referencing content only after all updates to that member are done.

**Operational rules (from BD support article 12000091105):**
- `send_email_notifications=1` — set this to trigger the welcome email (based on the membership plan's email settings). Without this flag, no email is sent — API creates are silent by default (since they typically aren't self-signups from the public site).
- **Email uniqueness — depends on the site setting `allow_duplicate_member_emails`:**
  - Setting OFF (default for most sites): BD REJECTS duplicate `email` on create — you'll get a validation error.
  - Setting ON: duplicate `email` values are ACCEPTED and stored. Identical `email` + `password` pairs are also accepted (no extra uniqueness check on the pair).
  - **The setting is not exposed via API**, so agents cannot pre-determine which behavior this site uses. **Recommended safe pattern:** call `listUsers` with `property=email&property_value=<email>&property_operator==` first and check for existing records before create. If a match exists, reuse that `user_id` (confirm with the user for imports) or call `updateUser` instead. This is idempotent — retries/partial-failure recovery don't create duplicates. (Alternative: attempt the create and handle any rejection as 'duplicate, fall back'; less safe because on a site with duplicates allowed, BOTH inserts succeed with different user_ids and the same email — live-verified 2026-04-19. A retry loop without pre-check creates orphan duplicates.)
- **`token`** (if supplied): must be exactly 32 alphanumeric characters AND unique across all members.
- **URL fields** (`website`, `booking_link`, `blog`, `facebook`, `twitter`, `linkedin`, `instagram`, etc.) are validated on write; invalid formats are silently skipped (not rejected). Must start with `http://` or `https://`.
- **Prerequisites:** `subscription_id` MUST reference an existing membership plan. For categories, existing IDs/names work by default; to auto-create unknown names during update, pass `create_new_categories=1` (see Category handling below).



**Category handling — IDs OR names, with sub-sub support (source: user.php:1840-1940, verified live 2026-04-18):**

*Top-level category (profession) — pick ONE:*
- `profession_id` (numeric) — must reference an existing row in `list_professions`
- `profession_name` (string) — alternative by name

*`services` parameter (sub-categories) supports 4 formats:*
| Format | Example | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| Single by ID or name | `services="1823"` or `services="PVC repair"` | One sub-category |
| Multiple | `services="PVC repair,Water heater"` or `services="1823,1824"` | Multiple subs |
| Sub + sub-sub via `=>` | `services="Honda=>2022"` | Creates sub "Honda" and sub-sub "2022" under Honda |
| Mixed subs + sub-subs | `services="Honda=>2022,Honda=>2023,Toyota"` | Combine in one call |

*Critical rules:*
- **`profession_id` (or `profession_name`) is REQUIRED when passing `services`** — without it, service relationships fail silently (`user.php:1876`)
- **Do NOT mix IDs and names** within a single `services` call — pick one format
- **Right side of `=>` is NAME-ONLY** — no ID lookup; sub-sub must be passed by name
- **Whitespace around commas is trimmed** — `"a, b, c"` works the same as `"a,b,c"`

*Auto-create on update: OFF by default.* Unknown category/service names are silently skipped unless you explicitly pass `create_new_categories=1`.

*Destructive side effect — `user.php:1832`:* **Changing `profession_id` on `updateUser` wipes all existing service (sub-category) relations for that member.** To move a member to a new top-level category without losing their sub-categories, re-send the complete `services` list in the same `updateUser` call.

member_tag_action.description: REQUIRED when modifying tags. Set to `1` alongside `member_tags` to actually apply the tag change — without this flag the `member_tags` value is ignored. Tag IDs not included in `member_tags` are removed; i.e. the submitted list REPLACES the current set (not additive). To fully clear tags, pass `member_tags` as an empty string with `member_tag_action=1`.

member_tags.description: Comma-separated tag IDs to assign to the member (e.g. `1,2,3`). Discover valid IDs via `listTags`. Requires `member_tag_action=1` to take effect. Replaces the existing tag set on save — include every tag you want the member to have, not just new additions. **Unknown tag IDs are silently dropped** — the response will echo your submitted `member_tags` value, but `tags` in the response will only reflect successfully-attached tags. Re-read the user after update and check the `tags` array to verify.

credit_action.description: How to apply `credit_amount` to the member's credit balance. `add` increments, `deduct` decrements, `override` REPLACES the balance with `credit_amount` (irreversible via API — no undo). Requires `credit_amount` to be set. Omit both fields to leave credits unchanged. BD does NOT reject deducts/overrides that produce a negative balance — if you need a non-negative guarantee, validate client-side against the current `credit_balance` (dollar-formatted string, may be negative) before calling.

credit_amount.description: Credit amount (number, may include decimals) paired with `credit_action`. Meaning depends on the action: for `add` and `deduct` it's the delta; for `override` it's the new absolute balance. Ignored if `credit_action` is not set.

images_action.description: Removes stored member images. `remove_all` clears all three (profile, logo, cover); the other values clear only the named image. PERMANENT via API — no undo. To REPLACE an image instead of removing, pass the new `profile_photo` / `logo` / `cover_photo` URL (optionally with `auto_image_import=1`) — do not set `images_action` for replacement.

services.description: Sub-categories for this member. Format: `category=>service1,service2` OR `service1,service2` (top category defaults to the member's current `profession_id`). WARNING: changing `profession_id` in the same call WIPES all existing sub-category links — re-send the full `services` list to preserve them.

auto_geocode.description: Use Google Maps to geocode this user's location. Requires the "Pretty URLs with Google Maps" feature to be enabled on the site. (string, "1" or "0").\n  1 = Yes\n  0 = No

active.description:
The status of the user. BD does NOT validate this field — integers outside the documented set are stored as-is (observed: `99`). Stick to documented values.

Documented values:
  1 = Not Active - Account Still Requires Activation
  2 = Active - Account is Live
  3 = Canceled - Sets User as Canceled Status
  4 = On Hold - Account Requires Moderation
  5 = Past Due - Sets User as Past Due Status
  6 = Incomplete - Paid signup hit an issue BD didn't account for; member was created but stays Incomplete instead of Active (without having paid). Rare edge state.

Reading: the top-level `status` response field ("Active", "Not Active", "Incomplete", etc.) is a computed label. When `active` is an unknown value, `status` is OMITTED from the response — do not treat `status` as always-present.

listing_type.description: Listing type classification. **BD does NOT validate this field — any string is stored verbatim.** Canonical values (exact case): `Individual` or `Company`. On `updateUser`, include only when reclassifying an existing member (e.g. fixing a record originally created as `Individual` that should be `Company`). If including, normalize case client-side — `individual` or other off-canonical casings will store as-is and break downstream logic. Otherwise omit to preserve the current value.

post.description:
Full-text search across members with category, location, and sorting options.

**Use when:** the user has a keyword / partial name / location / category and wants matching members. This is the full-text search backend that powers the public site's member-search page. For an exact-field lookup (by email, by user_id, by phone) use `listUsers` + `property`/`property_value` instead — it's faster and more precise.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` to paginate large result sets.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `q` — keyword (matches first name, last name, company, about, search_description)
- `pid` (category ID), `tid` (sub-category/service ID), `ttid` (sub-sub-category) — taxonomy filters, use IDs from `listTopCategories` / `listSubCategories`

**See also:** `getUser` (single record by ID), `listUsers` (full enumeration).

**Profile URL:** every user record has a `filename` field. To get the full public profile URL, concatenate: `<site-domain>/<user.filename>`. The `filename` is the complete relative path (e.g., `united-states/monterey-park/doctor/harrison-hasanuddin-d-o`) — DO NOT prepend `/business/`, `/profile/`, `/member/`, or any other segment. BD's router resolves `filename` verbatim.

Fetch the billing transaction history (invoices) for a specific member. Read-only. Backed by the WHMCS billing integration.

**Required:** `user_id` (POST body).

**Use when:** you need to see a member's paid/unpaid invoices, payment methods, billing history, or reconcile billing status. Common reasons: answering a member's "what did I pay for?" question, exporting billing history, auditing revenue per member.

**See also:** `getUserSubscriptions` (active/past membership plan signups — different resource from invoices), `getUser` (member profile).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: { total: <count>, invoices: [{...invoice records}] } }`. Each invoice includes `id`, `invoicenum` (may be empty string), `date`, `duedate`, `datepaid`, `subtotal`, `credit`, `tax`, `total`, `status` (`Paid`, `Unpaid`, etc.), `paymentmethod`, `notes` (admin-facing; may contain internal comments — redact before surfacing to end users), and an `items` array with per-line `description`, `amount`, `type`. NOT a simple list of rows — the `message` is an object containing `invoices` as the array. **Unpaid invoices** have `datepaid: "0000-00-00 00:00:00"` (MariaDB zero-date sentinel) — do NOT parse as ISO-8601; check `status === 'Unpaid'` or `datepaid.startsWith('0000')` first. `subscription_details` may be `false` (literal boolean) when absent.

**Reference support articles:**
- https://support.brilliantdirectories.com/support/solutions/articles/12000108047 (API Reference — Users)
- https://support.brilliantdirectories.com/support/solutions/articles/12000103947 (Users API — How to Manage Users with API Calls)

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: { total: <count>, subscriptions: [{...subscription records}] } }`. Each subscription includes `id`, `userid`, `packageid` (membership plan ID), `regdate`, `nextduedate`, `billingcycle` (e.g. `Monthly`, `Yearly`), `paymentmethod`, `amount`, `domainstatus` (`Active`, `Cancelled`, etc.), and related fields. send_lead_email_notification.description: Set to 1 to fire lead notification emails on create. Default is 0 — API-created leads are silent. With `auto_match=1` or `users_to_match` populated, the matched-member notification email fires. Without a match step, only the admin notification fires (if configured). For matched-member notifications you MUST combine this flag with a matching trigger (`auto_match=1` and/or `users_to_match`).

auto_match.description: Set to 1 to run the auto-match flow inline during create (equivalent to calling `matchLead` immediately after). Default is 0. When combined with `users_to_match`, the auto-match is bypassed and the lead is routed only to the specified members.

users_to_match.description: ADVANCED — OVERRIDES auto-match. Comma-separated list of member `user_id` values OR member email addresses to route this lead to directly. When provided, BD skips the normal category/location/service-area auto-matching and matches the lead to ONLY these members. Examples: `239` (single ID), `6099,6100` (multiple IDs), `user1@example.com,user2@example.com` (emails), mix allowed. Typically paired with `auto_match=1` to trigger the match step inline, and with `send_lead_email_notification=1` to fire the matched-member notification email — without that flag, matches are recorded but no email fires.

lead_status.description:
Lead status (integer). Authoritative — verified against BD admin UI dropdown on 2026-04-19:
  1 = Pending      (lead received, awaiting action)
  2 = Matched      (lead assigned to one or more members)
  4 = Follow-Up    (in progress, awaiting next touchpoint)
  5 = Sold Out     (no more capacity to fulfill this lead)

Returns custom fields for a specific post type form.

**Use when:** discovering the per-post-type custom fields before building a create/update payload. Pass `form_name` of the target post type (e.g. `blog_article_fields`, `events_fields`, etc.).

**Required:** `form_name`.

**Returns:** a BARE ARRAY of field-definition objects (NOT wrapped in `{status, message}`). Each entry has `key`, `label`, `required`, `type`, and optionally `choices` (for dropdown/radio), `default`, `helpText`.

**Silent-fallback warning:** if `form_name` does NOT match a real post-type form, BD returns HTTP 200 with a generic SUPER-UNION field list (containing every possible post field including `post_location`, `lat`, `lon`, `post_live_date`, `post_video`, `post_job`, internals like `post_type`/`logged_user`/`form_security_token`) — and `post_category.choices` will be ABSENT. Always verify `form_name` exists first via `listPostTypes` (look for the matching row's `form_name` column). If your response has `post_category` WITHOUT a `choices` array, you hit the fallback.

**Discovering `post_category` dropdown values:** `post_category` is a per-post-type dropdown whose options come from the post type's `feature_categories` CSV (admin-managed). Read `post_category.choices[].key` for the exact values to send. BD does NOT trim whitespace when splitting the CSV — options after the first may have a leading space (e.g. `" Category 2"`). Pass VERBATIM.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

parameters.name: form_name
parameters.description: Form slug for the post type

data_type.description: Classification family, read directly from the target post type's `data_type` column (via `listPostTypes` / `getPostType`). Values: `4` (multi-image — use `createMultiImagePost` instead), `9` (single-image video), `20` (single-image article/event/job/coupon). Internal-only classifications (`10`, `13`, `21`, `28`, `29`) are not post-creatable via this endpoint — use the resource-specific creator (e.g. `createReview` for `13`). Do NOT call `listDataTypes` — `data_type` is a classification, not a per-site FK.

post_caption.description: Pass raw HTML — do NOT wrap in `<![CDATA[...]]>`, do NOT escape as `<` / `>`. BD stores the field value verbatim; any wrapper/escape gets saved as literal text.

post_content.description: HTML allowed Pass raw HTML — do NOT wrap in `<![CDATA[...]]>`, do NOT escape as `<` / `>`. BD stores the field value verbatim; any wrapper/escape gets saved as literal text.

post_image.description: Feature image URL for the post. External URLs are supported natively; pair with `auto_image_import=1` to fetch and store locally.

post_category.description: Per-post-type dropdown value, configured in BD admin on the post type's `feature_categories` field. Discover allowed values via `getSingleImagePostFields(form_name=<post type's form_name>)` → `post_category.choices[].key`. Pass VERBATIM — BD does not trim whitespace (leading spaces after commas in `feature_categories` persist in the option values).

post_tags.description: Comma-separated keywords for the post. Free-form strings — not related to the `Tags` resource.

post_location.description: Full or partial street address for the post (Event, Coupon, Job, or any geo-enabled post type). Pair with `auto_geocode=1` to resolve to lat/lon automatically, or set `lat`/`lon`/`state_sn`/`country_sn` explicitly.

lat.description: Latitude for the post's location (geo-enabled post types). Decimal degrees as string.

lon.description: Longitude for the post's location (geo-enabled post types). Decimal degrees as string.

state_sn.description: 2-letter state/region code for the post's location (e.g. `CA`).

country_sn.description: 2-letter country code for the post's location (e.g. `US`).

post_live_date.description: Creation date stored on the post. Format: `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` (14-digit). Usually auto-set; override only for import/migration.

post_start_date.description: Publish / start date. REQUIRED for Event post types (marks when the event begins). Optional for others. Format: `YYYYMMDDHHmmss`.

post_expire_date.description: End/expiration date. Coupon post types use this for expiration; Event post types use it for end time. Format: `YYYYMMDDHHmmss`.

post_video.description: Full URL of a YouTube or Vimeo video. Only used by Video post types (`data_type=9`).

post_job.description: Employment type — used by Job post types only. Other post types ignore this field.

auto_geocode.description: Set to `1` to geocode the post's location via Google Maps (uses `post_location`/lat/lon/state_sn/country_sn if supplied). Requires the "Pretty URLs with Google Maps" site feature.

post_meta_title.description: SEO `<title>` override for the post's public page.

post_meta_description.description: SEO meta description override for the post's public page.

post_meta_keywords.description: SEO meta keywords (comma-separated) for the post's public page.

auto_image_import.description: **Auto-import images to site storage.** When any external image URL field is supplied on this single-image post (e.g. a feature image URL from a web-scrape), set `auto_image_import=1` to have BD fetch the image and save it locally. Without this flag, BD stores the URL as-is — images break if the source host goes down. **Recommended default when supplying external image URLs;** omit (or set `0`) only when the user explicitly wants to keep the external URL reference. Supports JPG/PNG/GIF/WebP AND SVG sources. Processing delay: a few minutes.

post.description:
Create a new post record. Writes live data.

**Use when:** creating a blog article, event, job listing, coupon, or any other single-image post type. Look up `data_id` + `data_type` via `listPostTypes` first — the post type's `data_type` field determines which create endpoint is correct. If `data_type=4` on the post type, use `createMultiImagePost` instead. For posts with scraped external image URLs, include `auto_image_import=1` to fetch and store them locally.

**Required:** `user_id`, `data_id`, `data_type`.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `user_id` — owner; must be an existing member (discover via `listUsers` or `searchUsers`)
- `data_id` — post type category ID; get via `listPostTypes`
- `data_type` — data type classification; usually matches the post type's data type
- `post_status`: `0`=Draft (not visible), `1`=Published (public)
- Response includes both `post_id` and `post_token` — the token is used for sharable URLs

**See also:** `updateSingleImagePost` (modify existing).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for bulk operations. Changes are immediately visible on the live site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` — includes the server-assigned ID (e.g., `user_id`, `post_id`). Use this ID for follow-up operations.

---

**Which endpoint to use — `data_type` family decides:**

Every post type in `data_categories` has a `data_type` field that classifies its family. Call `listPostTypes` or `getPostType` to see the `data_type` of your target post type, then choose:

| `data_type` value | Family | Use endpoint |
|---|---|---|
| `4` | Multi-image (albums, galleries, photo-heavy listings — e.g. Classified, Photo Album, Property, Product) | `createMultiImagePost` |
| `9` | Single-image video | `createSingleImagePost` |
| `20` | Single-image article / event / blog / job / coupon | `createSingleImagePost` |
| `10`, `13`, `21`, `28`, `29` (and similar) | Internal admin types (Member Listings, Reviews, Sub Accounts, Specialties, Favorites) — NOT posts | Use the resource-specific endpoint (e.g. `createReview` for `data_type=13`) |

If you call the wrong create endpoint for a given post type, BD may accept the row but it won't render on the public site correctly.

**For "make a blog post" intent:** look up `data_categories` for `data_name` matching "blog" (commonly `data_id=14` with `data_type=20`) → `createSingleImagePost` with that `data_id` + `data_type`.

**For "make a photo album" / "gallery" intent:** look up the album post type (often `data_id=10`, `data_type=4`) → `createMultiImagePost` with that `data_id` + `data_type`. Photos are added separately via `createMultiImagePostPhoto` using the returned `group_id`.

**Picking `post_category` (and other per-type dropdowns):** `post_category` values are configured PER POST TYPE by the site admin in the post type's `feature_categories` CSV. Before create, call `getSingleImagePostFields(form_name=<post type's form_name>)` and read `post_category.choices[].key`. Pass ONLY values from that list, VERBATIM — BD does not trim whitespace when splitting `feature_categories`, so options after the first may have a leading space (e.g. `" Category 2"`). If the user names a category that isn't in the list: ask whether to pick the closest existing option or have them add the new option in BD admin first — do NOT invent a new value. WARNING: if `form_name` does not match a real post type form, `getSingleImagePostFields` silently returns a generic SUPER-UNION field list (HTTP 200, no error) — verify `form_name` exists in `listPostTypes` first.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

post_content.description: Pass raw HTML — do NOT wrap in `<![CDATA[...]]>`, do NOT escape as `<` / `>`. BD stores the field value verbatim; any wrapper/escape gets saved as literal text.

post_status.description:
Post publication status (integer).
  0 = Draft      (saved but not publicly visible)
  1 = Published  (publicly visible on the site)
Default: depends on BD site settings; typically 0 (draft) for safety.

post_image.description: Feature image URL. Pair with `auto_image_import=1` to fetch external URLs into site storage.

post_category.description: Per-post-type dropdown value. Discover allowed values via `getSingleImagePostFields`. Pass VERBATIM (BD does not trim whitespace).

auto_geocode.description: Set to `1` to geocode the post's location. Requires the "Pretty URLs with Google Maps" site feature.

post_location.description: Full or partial street address (Event/Coupon/Job/geo-enabled post types).

post_start_date.description: Format: `YYYYMMDDHHmmss`. Event post types: start time.

post_expire_date.description: Format: `YYYYMMDDHHmmss`. Coupon: expiration. Event: end time.

post_video.description: YouTube/Vimeo URL — Video post types only.

post_job.description: Employment type — Job post types only.

auto_image_import.description: Set to `1` to fetch external image URLs in `post_image` into site storage. Supports JPG/PNG/GIF/WebP and SVG.

put.description:
Update an existing post record by ID. Fields omitted are untouched. Writes live data.

**Use when:** editing post content, switching from draft to published (`post_status=0`→`1`), updating post title/caption, or correcting post metadata. To move a post to a different post type (rare), pass `data_id` — but validate the new post type is still in the single-image family.

**Required:** `post_id`.

**Enums:** `post_status`: `0`=Draft (saved but not publicly visible), `1`=Published (publicly visible on the site).

**See also:** `createSingleImagePost` (add new), `deleteSingleImagePost` (remove permanently).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for bulk operations. Changes are immediately visible on the live site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...updatedRecord} }` — the full updated record after changes applied.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

delete.description:
Permanently delete a post record by ID. Destructive — cannot be undone via API.

**Use when:** removing a post permanently. For "hide without deleting" use `updateSingleImagePost` with `post_status=0` (Draft). Deleting also removes the post_token, breaking any external links to the share URL.

**Required:** `post_id`.

**See also:** `updateSingleImagePost` (modify without removing).

**Destructive:** confirm intent with the user before bulk use. No soft-delete via API — records removed are not recoverable.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: "record was deleted" }`. No body beyond the confirmation string.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

post.description:
Keyword search across post records. Read-only.

**Use when:** an admin or agent wants to find a post by keyword or title. Pass `data_id` to scope search to one post type — strongly recommended since results depend on per-post-type configuration.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` to paginate large result sets.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `data_id` (post type) is strongly recommended — results vary significantly by post type configuration
- `q` — keyword across post title, caption, content
- Fields exposed in search depend on the post type; use `getSingleImagePostFields` with `form_name` to discover them

**See also:** `getSingleImagePost` (single record by ID), `listSingleImagePosts` (full enumeration).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: [...records] }`. Supports pagination fields when result set is large.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

get.description:
Paginated enumeration of portfoliogroup records. Read-only.

**Use when:** enumerating photo-album / gallery-style posts (Photo Album, Classified, Property, Product — any post type with `data_type=4`). For single-image post types use `listSingleImagePosts`.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` token from the previous response's `next_page`. Do not assume integer offsets.

**Filter/sort:** `property` + `property_value` + `property_operator` (`=`, `LIKE`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`) to filter; `order_column` + `order_type` (`ASC`/`DESC`) to sort.

**See also:** `getMultiImagePost` (single record by ID), `searchMultiImagePosts` (keyword search).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", total, current_page, total_pages, next_page, prev_page, message: [...records] }`. Each record is the full resource object.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

Fetch a single portfoliogroup record. Read-only.

**Use when:** fetching one multi-image post by `group_id`. Photos in this post are loaded separately via `listMultiImagePostPhotos` with `group_id` filter.

**Required:** `group_id`.

**See also:** `listMultiImagePosts` (enumerate many), `searchMultiImagePosts` (keyword search).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: [{...record}] }` — the `message` array contains 1 record when found. Empty or HTTP 404 when not found.

Fetch field definitions for a multi-image post type form. Read-only.

**Use when:** discovering per-post-type custom fields for multi-image posts — same pattern as `getSingleImagePostFields` but for the `users_portfolio_groups` resource.

**Required:** `form_name`.

**Returns:** a BARE ARRAY of field-definition objects (NOT wrapped in `{status, message}`). Each entry has `key`, `label`, `required`, `type`, and optionally `choices`, `default`, `helpText`. Multi-image post fields seen: `user_id`, `group_status`, `group_name`, `group_desc`, `post_image` (CSV of image URLs), `auto_image_import`, `post_tags`, `auto_geocode`. Categorization for multi-image posts is exposed under an internal widget-controller field name, not a clean `post_category` — not straightforward to write via API.

**Silent-fallback warning:** if `form_name` does NOT match a real post-type form, BD may return a generic field list without error. Verify `form_name` exists in `listPostTypes` before trusting the response.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

data_type.description: Classification family — for this endpoint, must be `4` (multi-image). Read from the target post type's `data_type` column via `listPostTypes` / `getPostType`. If the post type's `data_type` is `9` or `20`, use `createSingleImagePost` instead. Do NOT call `listDataTypes` — `data_type` is a classification, not a per-site FK.

post_image.description: Comma-separated list of image URLs to import as the album's photos (created at upload time as child `MultiImagePostPhoto` records). Pair with `auto_image_import=1` to fetch externals into site storage. For finer control (per-photo title, status), omit this and add photos one-by-one via `createMultiImagePostPhoto` after the album is created.

post_tags.description: Comma-separated keywords for the album. Free-form strings — not related to the `Tags` resource.

auto_geocode.description: Set to `1` to geocode the album's location. Requires the "Pretty URLs with Google Maps" site feature.

group_desc.description: Pass raw HTML — do NOT wrap in `<![CDATA[...]]>`, do NOT escape as `<` / `>`. BD stores the field value verbatim; any wrapper/escape gets saved as literal text.

auto_image_import.description: **Auto-import images to site storage.** When any external image URL field is supplied on this multi-image post (e.g. a feature image URL from a web-scrape), set `auto_image_import=1` to have BD fetch the image and save it locally. Without this flag, BD stores the URL as-is — images break if the source host goes down. **Recommended default when supplying external image URLs;** omit (or set `0`) only when the user explicitly wants to keep the external URL reference. Supports JPG/PNG/GIF/WebP AND SVG sources. Processing delay: a few minutes.

post.description:
Create a new portfoliogroup record. Writes live data.

**Use when:** creating a photo album, gallery, product listing with multiple photos, or any post type with `data_type=4`. Confirm the target post type's `data_type` via `listPostTypes` first — `data_type=4` belongs here; 9/20 belongs in `createSingleImagePost`. Bulk-add photos at create time via `post_image` (CSV of URLs) + `auto_image_import=1`, or add them one-by-one after create via `createMultiImagePostPhoto` using the returned `group_id`.

**Required:** `user_id`, `data_id`, `data_type`.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `data_id` + `data_type` — specify the post type this album belongs to (from `listPostTypes`; `data_type` must be `4`)
- `group_status`: `0`=Hidden, `1`=Published
- `post_image` — comma-separated image URLs imported as child photos at create time
- `auto_image_import=1` — fetches the `post_image` URLs into site storage (required for external sources to survive)

**See also:** `updateMultiImagePost` (modify existing), `createMultiImagePostPhoto` (add photos individually).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for bulk operations. Changes are immediately visible on the live site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` — includes the server-assigned `group_id`. Use it for follow-up photo adds.

---

**Which endpoint to use — `data_type` family decides:**

Every post type in `data_categories` has a `data_type` field that classifies its family. Call `listPostTypes` or `getPostType` to see the `data_type` of your target post type, then choose:

| `data_type` value | Family | Use endpoint |
|---|---|---|
| `4` | Multi-image (albums, galleries, photo-heavy listings — e.g. Classified, Photo Album, Property, Product) | `createMultiImagePost` |
| `9` | Single-image video | `createSingleImagePost` |
| `20` | Single-image article / event / blog / job / coupon | `createSingleImagePost` |
| `10`, `13`, `21`, `28`, `29` (and similar) | Internal admin types (Member Listings, Reviews, Sub Accounts, Specialties, Favorites) — NOT posts | Use the resource-specific endpoint (e.g. `createReview` for `data_type=13`) |

If you call the wrong create endpoint for a given post type, BD may accept the row but it won't render on the public site correctly.

**Category for multi-image posts:** multi-image posts do NOT expose `post_category` like single-image posts do. Album-level categorization is configured differently in BD admin and is not cleanly writable via the create payload. If categorization is needed, add it via a follow-up `updateMultiImagePost` or BD admin.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

get.description:
Paginated enumeration of posttype records. Read-only.

**Use when:** discovering which post types exist on this site AND their `data_type` families. The `data_type` value on each row tells you whether a post type belongs to `createSingleImagePost` (9/20) or `createMultiImagePost` (4). Use this BEFORE calling either create endpoint to pick the correct tool. **Also the standard discovery step for the Member Listings post type** (singleton per site, `data_type=10`, `system_name=member_listings`): filter `property=data_type&property_value=10&property_operator==` to retrieve the single record; the `data_id` varies per site. Member Listings controls the member search results page UI/UX + profile/detail page — see `updatePostType` description for editable fields, code-group save rules, and master-fallback behavior.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` token from the previous response's `next_page`. Do not assume integer offsets.

**Filter/sort:** `property` + `property_value` + `property_operator` (`=`, `LIKE`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`) to filter; `order_column` + `order_type` (`ASC`/`DESC`) to sort.

**Payload size — filter, don't enumerate blindly:** each row has ~90 fields, and on sites with customized post types, layout fields (`search_results_layout`, `profile_results_layout`, `search_code_hidden`, `detail_page_code_hidden`) can embed entire PHP templates — responses have been seen at 80k+ chars. BD does not offer a `fields=` response-shaping parameter. The practical fix is to filter and cap:
- If you know the target post type name, filter: `property=data_name&property_value=Blog&property_operator=LIKE&limit=5`
- If you know the `data_id` already, skip this entirely — use `getPostType` (single record)
- For "discover blog-family post types": filter by `data_type` (`=4` multi-image, `=20` single-image article/blog/event) with a small `limit` to see only the relevant family
- For full enumeration for site-mapping, set `limit=100` and paginate; expect to burn tokens on a site with many customized types

**See also:** `getPostType` (single record by ID).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", total, current_page, total_pages, next_page, prev_page, message: [...records] }`. Each record is the full resource object.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

Fetch a single posttype record. Read-only.

**Use when:** checking the configuration of one post type (which `data_type` family, whether active, custom field config, current search-results / profile-page template code). Commonly followed by `getPostTypeCustomFields` to enumerate per-type fields. **Also the canonical read before any `updatePostType` code-field edit** — see master-fallback note below.

**Required:** `data_id`.

**⚠️ Code-field master-fallback:** the up to eight HTML/PHP code fields on every post type record (`category_header`, `search_results_div`, `category_footer`, `profile_header`, `profile_results_layout`, `profile_footer`, `search_results_layout`, `comments_code`) begin life backed by the BD-core master template and only persist locally in the site DB when an admin (or API call) saves them. This endpoint returns the MASTER value for any code field that has no local override — so the agent always sees the real rendered code, not an empty string. This matters because any edit to one of the grouped code fields (search-results group = header+loop+footer, profile group = header+body+footer) MUST include all fields in that group on the write (see `updatePostType` description for the all-or-nothing save rule). Always read current values here BEFORE calling `updatePostType`.

**See also:** `listPostTypes` (enumerate many; discover Member Listings via `data_type=10` filter), `updatePostType` (write; carries the full code-group save rules and Member Listings workflow), `getPostTypeCustomFields` (per-type custom field enum).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: [{...record}] }` — the `message` array contains 1 record when found. Empty or HTTP 404 when not found.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

post.summary: Get custom fields for a post type
post.description:
Fetch a single posttypecustomfields record. Read-only.

**Use when:** building a create/update payload for a post type that has custom fields (most do). Returns the exact per-type schema to send. Pass `data_id` of the target post type.

**Required:** `data_id`.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `data_id` — the post type to introspect; get via `listPostTypes`
- Returns custom field definitions specific to this post type — use to build create/update payloads for matching posts

**Discovering enumerated field values (e.g. `post_category`):** per-post-type dropdowns like `post_category` are configured by the site admin and live in this schema. There is NO `createPostCategory` API tool — if the user needs a new dropdown option, that is admin-side work. Call this before a create/update to see the exact allowed values for select/radio/checkbox fields, and pass only those values verbatim.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: [{...record}] }` — the `message` array contains 1 record when found. Empty or HTTP 404 when not found.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

data_id.description: Post type primary key. Required. For the Member Listings post type (`data_type=10`, singleton per site), discover via `listPostTypes` filtered by `property=data_type&property_value=10&property_operator==` — `data_id` varies per site.

category_tab.description: Admin-UI label for the post-type tab.

per_page.description: Number of results per search results page. Default 9 (Member Listings). Recommended max 500 for site-speed reasons.

keyword_search_filter.description: Post Keyword Search Options. `level_2` = default fields only (faster). `level_3` = default + custom fields (slower; sites with many posts/custom fields can be significantly slower). Default on Member Listings: `level_3`.

enableLazyLoad.description: Pagination Display Options. `1` = Insta-Load Search Results (default on Member Listings), `0` = Standard Pagination, `2` = Hide Pagination.

category_order_by.description: Display order of results. `alphabet-asc` (Member Name A-Z, default) / `alphabet-desc` / `userid-asc` (Member ID Oldest First) / `userid-desc` (Newest First) / `last_name_asc` / `last_name_desc` / `reviews` (Most Reviews First) / `random`.

category_ignore_search_priority.description: When sorting members, respect Membership Plan 'Search Priority'? `0` = Yes (respect plan priority, default), `1` = No (ignore plan priority).

post_type_cache_system.description: Enable search-results cache. `1` = Yes (recommended; subsequent matching searches load faster). `0` = No (always query DB). **Cannot be `1` when `category_order_by=random`** — the admin UI disables the cache in that case; if you send `post_type_cache_system=1` with `category_order_by=random`, the combination is invalid.

category_sidebar.description: Sidebar to display on search results pages. Valid values: empty string (no sidebar), one of the 6 Master Default Sidebars (`Global Website Search`, `Member Profile Page`, `Member Search Result`, `Personal Post Feed`, `Post Search Result`, `Post Single Page`), or any custom sidebar returned by `listSidebars` on this specific site. **Semantic equivalent of `form_name` on WebPages — different variable name, same value set.** Default on Member Listings out-of-the-box: `Member Search Result`.

sidebar_search_module.description: Widget name for the search module rendered inside the sidebar. Must match an existing widget on the site. Common values include `Bootstrap Theme - Search Module - Keyword_Location` (default on Member Listings), `No Search Module - None` (disables), `Bootstrap Theme - Search Module - Keyword Only`, `Bootstrap Theme - Search Module - Local Radius Search`, `Bootstrap Theme - Search Module - Top Category Only`, `Bootstrap Theme - Search Module - Top_Category_Location`, `Bootstrap Theme - Search Module - Top_Sub_Category`, plus the full Dynamic Category Filter set and post-search module variants. Full enum not enforced server-side — BD adds new widgets in core releases. If unsure which value is valid on THIS site, enumerate current widgets via `listWidgets` and match by `widget_name`. Also: the underlying sidebar chosen in `category_sidebar` must contain the `Bootstrap Theme - Search Module - Dynamic Sidebar Search` host widget for the chosen search-module widget to render.

sidebar_position_mobile.description: Sidebar position on mobile devices ONLY (desktop uses `menu_layout`/page-level defaults). `top` = above results, `bottom` = below results (default), `hide` = do not render sidebar on mobile.

enable_search_results_map.description: Display Google Map option at top of search results pages. `1` = Yes, show the map pin icon (default on Member Listings). `0` = No. Requires the Google Maps site feature to be enabled for the map itself to load.

category_header.description: **CODE FIELD — search results HEADER** (HTML/CSS/JS/iframe/PHP supported; widget-equivalent trust level — no input sanitization applied). Renders ABOVE the member-search results loop on the public page. Supports PHP variables (e.g. `<?php echo $user_data['full_name']; ?>`) and BD text labels `%%%text_label%%%`. ⚠️ **Master-fallback on read + all-or-nothing save on write** — see post-type-write-rules in tool description.

search_results_div.description: **CODE FIELD — search results LOOP** (HTML/CSS/JS/iframe/PHP supported; widget-equivalent trust level — no input sanitization applied). Renders ONCE PER matching member in the results list. Supports PHP variables and `%%%text_label%%%` tokens. ⚠️ **Part of the search-results code group** with `category_header` + `category_footer` — see all-or-nothing save rule in tool description.

category_footer.description: **CODE FIELD — search results FOOTER** (HTML/CSS/JS/iframe/PHP supported; widget-equivalent trust level — no input sanitization applied). Renders BELOW the member-search results loop. Supports PHP variables and `%%%text_label%%%` tokens. ⚠️ **Part of the search-results code group** with `category_header` + `search_results_div` — see all-or-nothing save rule in tool description.

profile_header.description: **CODE FIELD — profile/detail page HEADER** (HTML/CSS/JS/iframe/PHP; widget-equivalent trust). Renders ABOVE the main content on the single-record detail page (for Member Listings: the member profile page). ⚠️ **Part of the profile code group** with `profile_results_layout` + `profile_footer` — see all-or-nothing save rule.

profile_results_layout.description: **CODE FIELD — profile/detail page BODY** (HTML/CSS/JS/iframe/PHP; widget-equivalent trust). The main detail-page template — BD's equivalent of a WordPress `single.php`. Name is misleading: this is the DETAIL page for a single record, not a search-results layout. For Member Listings, this drives the member profile page. ⚠️ **Part of the profile code group** with `profile_header` + `profile_footer` — see all-or-nothing save rule.

profile_footer.description: **CODE FIELD — profile/detail page FOOTER** (HTML/CSS/JS/iframe/PHP; widget-equivalent trust). Renders BELOW the main content on the single-record detail page. ⚠️ **Part of the profile code group** with `profile_header` + `profile_results_layout` — see all-or-nothing save rule.

search_results_layout.description: **CODE FIELD — single-record detail page code** (HTML/CSS/JS/iframe/PHP; widget-equivalent trust). ⚠️ **Misleading variable name** — despite the `search_results_` prefix, this is the DETAIL page code for a single post record (BD's equivalent of WordPress `single.php`). Applies ONLY to post types that have a per-record detail page (blog, event, coupon, property, product, etc.). **NOT applicable to the Member Listings post type (`data_type=10`)** — members render via BD's core member profile system, not via a post-type template. Do not send this field on Member Listings updates. Standalone — not grouped with the profile triplet; saves independently. Uses master-fallback-on-read like the other code fields.

comments_code.description: **CODE FIELD — additional detail-page footer/embed code** (HTML/CSS/JS/iframe/PHP; widget-equivalent trust). Renders directly AFTER `search_results_layout` on the single-record detail page — intended for embed widgets, schema markup, analytics pixels, structured data, or any auxiliary code that needs to live at the bottom of the detail page. Applies ONLY to post types with a per-record detail page (blog, event, coupon, property, product, etc.). **NOT applicable to the Member Listings post type (`data_type=10`)** — Member Listings has no post-type-driven detail page. Do not send this field on Member Listings updates. Standalone — not grouped with the profile triplet or with `search_results_layout`; saves independently. Uses master-fallback-on-read like the other code fields.

put.description:
Update an existing posttype record by ID. Fields omitted are untouched (for most fields — see code-group exception below). Writes live data.

**Use when:** toggling a post type active/inactive, renaming, changing per-page display counts, editing search-results UI or profile-page code, or (for Member Listings specifically) tuning keyword-search mode, pagination display, sidebar, or sort-order settings. For custom field definitions on a post type, work in the BD admin UI.

**Required:** `data_id`.

**⚠️ MEMBER LISTINGS SPECIAL CASE (`data_type=10`):** Every BD site has exactly one post type with `data_type=10` (system_name `member_listings`) — it controls the Member Search Results page UI/UX. **Unlike every other post type, Member Listings has NO profile/detail page of its own** — members already render via the normal member profile system (which is NOT a post-type template). As a result, on Member Listings ONLY the search-results surface is editable; the profile code group (`profile_header`/`profile_results_layout`/`profile_footer`) and the standalone `search_results_layout` field are NOT editable and must NOT be sent — they have no effect on Member Listings rendering. The `data_id` varies per site. **Before calling this endpoint** for Member Listings work, discover the record: `listPostTypes` with `property=data_type&property_value=10&property_operator==`. Cache the returned `data_id` for the session (it never changes for a site). **Editable fields for Member Listings (the complete closed set):** settings = `h1`, `h2`, `per_page`, `keyword_search_filter`, `enableLazyLoad`, `category_order_by`, `category_ignore_search_priority`, `post_type_cache_system`, `category_sidebar`, `sidebar_search_module`, `sidebar_position_mobile`, `enable_search_results_map`; code fields = `category_header`, `search_results_div`, `category_footer` (the search-results group — all-or-nothing save rule applies, see below). Nothing else. Do NOT mutate structural fields on Member Listings (`data_name`, `system_name`, `data_type`, `data_active`, `data_filename`, `form_name`, `icon`, `software_version`, `display_order`) — these are set by BD system seeding and changing them can break site rendering. Member Listings `data_active` should always be `1`; no legitimate reason to disable it via API.

**⚠️ CODE FIELDS — master-fallback on GET + all-or-nothing save per group.** Up to eight code-template fields on a post-type record begin life backed by the MASTER post-type template (shared BD-core defaults) and only persist locally in the site DB when an admin (or API call) saves them. On `getPostType`/`listPostTypes`, BD returns the MASTER value for any code field that has no local override yet — so the agent always sees the real rendered code, not an empty string. On WRITE, fields in the same group save atomically: **if you update ANY field in a group, you MUST send all fields in that group** (preserving unchanged values from the prior GET). Writing only some group members causes the omitted ones to drift back to master on the next render cycle — the site will appear to lose customizations. The groups and which post types they apply to:
  1. **Search-results group (every post type INCLUDING Member Listings):** `category_header` + `search_results_div` + `category_footer`. On edit: send all three. This is the ONLY code group on Member Listings.
  2. **Profile/detail group (post types WITH a per-record detail page — blog, event, coupon, property, product, etc. — NOT Member Listings):** `profile_header` + `profile_results_layout` + `profile_footer`. On edit: send all three. DO NOT send these three on Member Listings — they have no effect there.
  3. **Standalone fields (post types WITH a per-record detail page — NOT Member Listings):** `search_results_layout` (detail-page wrapper, BD's `single.php` analogue — misleading name) and `comments_code` (auxiliary footer code rendered directly after `search_results_layout`, used for embeds/schema markup/pixels). Both save independently — no group rule. Uses master-fallback-on-read like the grouped code fields. DO NOT send either on Member Listings.

**Standard code-edit workflow:**
  1. `getPostType(data_id=<found>)` — returns current values, falling back to master for any un-customized code fields.
  2. Identify which group the user's change belongs to (search-results, profile, or standalone).
  3. Build the update payload: the CHANGED field(s) with new values + the OTHER fields in the same group copied verbatim from the GET response.
  4. `updatePostType` with `data_id` + the full group.
  5. `refreshSiteCache` — post-type edits are cached and won't reflect publicly until refreshed. Always call after any successful `updatePostType`, even for non-code settings — cheap safety.

**Code-field trust level:** `category_header`, `search_results_div`, `category_footer`, `profile_header`, `profile_results_layout`, `profile_footer`, `search_results_layout`, `comments_code` are widget-equivalent — they accept arbitrary HTML, CSS, JS, iframes, and PHP (BD evaluates PHP server-side at render). Input sanitization rules (XSS/SQLi patterns) do NOT apply to these fields; anyone with API permission to edit post-type code already has full site code control. Supports PHP variables (e.g. `<?php echo $user_data['full_name']; ?>`) and BD text-label tokens (`%%%text_label%%%`) in templates.

**See also:** `getPostType` (single), `listPostTypes` (discover by `data_type`), `deletePostType` (remove permanently — NOT for Member Listings; system-required).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for any code-field edit on Member Listings — these changes affect every member-search page on the site. Changes go live on the next cache refresh.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...updatedRecord} }` — the full updated record after changes applied.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._
