Permanently delete a widget by `widget_id`. Destructive — cannot be undone via API.

**Use when:** removing an unused widget. For "disable without deleting" use `updateWidget` with `widget_viewport=admin` (hides from public pages) — preserves the source for later use.

**Destructive caveat:** any page or email using `[widget=Name]` shortcode referencing the deleted widget will render as empty or broken at that spot. Audit with `listWidgets` + check page content for shortcodes referencing this widget's `widget_name` or `short_code` before deleting.

**Required:** `widget_id`.

**See also:** `updateWidget` with `widget_viewport=admin` (reversible hide).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: "data_widgets record was deleted" }`.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

post.description:
Return the RENDERED HTML output of a widget with all template variables processed. Read-only from the widget's perspective, but may have side effects (see below). **Use when:** previewing a widget's output during editing, embedding rendered BD widget content on an EXTERNAL site (the main real-world use case — render-on-request for external embeds), or scraping a widget's current state for caching/screenshot purposes. For fetching the raw SOURCE code instead, use `getWidget`. **Required:** either `widget_id` OR `widget_name` (pass one — both are supported per BD docs for lookup). **Prerequisite from BD docs:** *"The widget needs to be customized to get the widget output."* — if the widget hasn't been customized/saved at least once in the BD admin, this endpoint may return empty output. Verify with `getWidget` first if rendering returns nothing. **Side effects:** rendering executes widget-specific PHP server-side — may trigger database queries, cache lookups, third-party API calls, or counter increments depending on what the widget does. Use carefully inside loops or batch operations. **See also:** `getWidget` (raw source), `listWidgets` (discover widgets), `updateWidget` (edit then re-render). **Returns (distinct from standard envelope):** ``` { "status": "success", "message": "Widget rendered successfully", "name": "My Widget Name", "output": "
...fully rendered HTML...
" } ``` Note: `name` and `output` are TOP-LEVEL siblings of `message`, NOT nested inside it. The `output` field contains the full rendered HTML including any `widget_data` HTML + expanded template tokens. CSS and JS declared on the widget are NOT included in `output` — fetch them separately via `getWidget` if needed for external embedding. **Error response shape:** `{ "status": "error", "message": "Widget not found", "name": "", "output": "" }`. When the widget doesn't exist or isn't customized, expect empty `name` and `output` strings. **Example use case — rendering a BD widget on an external site:** ``` 1. createWidget on BD with your HTML/CSS/JS 2. On external site, fetch via POST /api/v2/data_widgets/render with widget_id 3. Parse the response's 'output' field → inject into external page DOM 4. If you need the CSS/JS, fetch via getWidget and include separately ``` _Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

post.description:
Create a new emailtemplate record. Writes live data.

**Use when:** adding a new transactional/marketing template. Rare — most BD email templates are built into the admin UI.

**Required:** `email_name`.

**Enums:** `signature`: `0`, `1`; `notemplate`: `0`, `1`; `unsubscribe_link`: `0`, `1`.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `email_subject` and `email_body` can use template tokens (e.g. `%%%website_name%%%`, recipient field tokens)
- `email_body` supports HTML

**See also:** `updateEmailTemplate` (modify existing).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for bulk operations. Changes are immediately visible on the live site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` — includes the server-assigned ID (e.g., `user_id`, `post_id`). Use this ID for follow-up operations.

form_action.description: **post** = form submits via POST body (default, most forms). **get** = form submits as URL query string (use for bookmarkable search/filter forms).

form_layout.description: **bootstrapvertical** = Labels Above Inputs (default). **bootstrap** = Labels Left of Inputs.

form_table.description: Database table the form submissions post into. Default: `website_contacts`. Override only when building a form that posts into a different BD table.

form_email_on.description: Send admin notification email on each submission. `0` = OFF (default when an agent creates a form — safer: spammy forms won't flood the admin inbox). `1` = ON. Admin UI defaults this to ON; agents default it to OFF unless user explicitly requests notifications.

form_action_type.description: What happens after a successful submit. `widget` = Display Success Pop-Up Message (default on agent create). `notification` = Display Success Alert Message (inline banner). `redirect` = Redirect to Another Page — requires `form_target` (destination URL) to be set. `""` (empty) = no post-submit behavior defined; valid for some internal/programmatic forms but NOT for public-facing user forms. **When this field is set to `widget`, `notification`, or `redirect`, the form's fields list MUST end with a `ReCaptcha` field, then a `HoneyPot` field, then a `Button` (Submit) field — in that order. Without all three at the end, BD errors on submit and the form won't function.** See `createFormField`.

form_target.description: Destination URL. **Required when `form_action_type=redirect`; ignored otherwise.** Full URL (e.g. `https://mysite.com/thanks`). BD validates URL shape; malformed URLs cause the redirect to silently fail on submit.

form_url.description: Save Action URL — advanced setting that overrides the default `action=` attribute on the rendered HTML form. **Required exact value for every form an agent creates:** `/api/widget/json/post/Bootstrap%20Theme%20-%20Function%20-%20Save%20Form`. This is the BD Save Form widget endpoint; without it, the submit wiring breaks. Do NOT decode the `%20` characters — BD needs the URL-encoded form verbatim.

table_index.description: Unique field identifier column for the `form_table`. **Required exact value for every form an agent creates:** `ID`. This tells BD which column is the primary key on the submissions table. Without it, BD can't look up or update individual submission records.

post.description:
Create a new form record. Writes live data. Add fields afterward via `createFormField`.

**Required:** `form_name`, `form_title`, `form_action`, `form_layout`, `form_table`.

**⚠️ Required form recipe (must follow for the form to submit without errors):**

1. **`form_url`** — set to the exact value `/api/widget/json/post/Bootstrap%20Theme%20-%20Function%20-%20Save%20Form`. This is the BD Save Form widget endpoint; without it the form's `action=` attribute is wrong and the submit won't wire up. URL-encoded spaces (`%20`) must stay encoded.
2. **`table_index`** — set to the exact value `ID`. Tells BD which column is the primary key on the submissions table.
3. **`form_action_type`** — set to `widget` (Display Success Pop-Up Message — safe default) unless the user specifies `notification` or `redirect`. Never leave empty for a public-facing form.
4. **`form_email_on`** — set to `0` (OFF) unless the user explicitly asks for admin notification emails. Admin UI defaults this to ON; agent default is OFF to avoid spammy-form-floods-admin-inbox.
5. **If `form_action_type=redirect`** — set `form_target=<destination URL>`.
6. **When `form_action_type` is `widget` / `notification` / `redirect`**, the form's **fields list MUST end with these three fields in this order** (add via `createFormField` with ascending `field_order`):
   - `field_type=ReCaptcha` — Google reCAPTCHA challenge
   - `field_type=HoneyPot` — invisible spam trap
   - `field_type=Button` — the actual Submit button

**Without every rule above, BD errors on submit and the form won't function.** The ReCaptcha/HoneyPot fields need no configuration beyond `field_type` — BD handles everything else server-side.

**Field defaults and valid values:**
- `form_action` — `post` (default) or `get` (use `get` only for bookmarkable search/filter forms).
- `form_layout` — `bootstrapvertical` (default; Labels Above Inputs) or `bootstrap` (Labels Left of Inputs).
- `form_table` — submissions table, default `website_contacts`. Override only for custom workflows.
- `form_action_type` — `widget` (default) / `notification` / `redirect` / `""` (empty, internal-only).
- `form_target` — required when `form_action_type=redirect`, otherwise ignored.
- `form_email_on` — `0` (default) / `1` (enable notification emails to admin).

**See also:** `updateForm` (modify existing), `createFormField` (add fields), `listFormFields` (inspect existing forms).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for bulk operations. Changes are immediately visible on the live site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` — includes the server-assigned form identifier.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

form_email_on.description: Send admin notification email on each submission. `0` = OFF, `1` = ON.

form_action_type.description: Post-submit behavior. `widget` = success pop-up, `notification` = success alert banner, `redirect` = send user to `form_target` URL, `""` = no behavior (internal-only forms). **If changing TO `redirect`, also set `form_target`. If changing FROM empty to any of `widget`/`notification`/`redirect` on an existing form, verify the fields list ends with `ReCaptcha` → `HoneyPot` → `Button` (in that order) — otherwise submits will error.**

form_target.description: Destination URL, required when `form_action_type=redirect`, ignored otherwise. Full URL with `https://`.

form_url.description: Save Action URL. Canonical value: `/api/widget/json/post/Bootstrap%20Theme%20-%20Function%20-%20Save%20Form`. If a form was created via this API with the correct value, leave this field alone on update — only touch it to repair a form that was created without it.

Verify via `listFormFields` before changing `form_action_type` — if the tail pattern is missing, the form will error on submit. Add missing fields via `createFormField`.

post.description:
Create a new formfield record. Writes live data.

**Use when:** adding an input to an existing form.

**Required:** `form_name`, `field_name`, `field_text`, `field_type`, `field_order`.

**Enums:** `field_required`: `0`, `1`.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `form_name` — the parent form slug (from `listForms` / `createForm`). Note: only `form_name` is accepted; there is no `form_id` parameter on this endpoint.
- Field type and options depend on the form type; validate against the form's field schema

**Limitation — dropdown option lists:** `Checkbox`, `Select`, `Radio`, and `YesNo` field types accept their type value on create but there is NO API parameter to supply their option list (no `field_options`/`choices`/`enum` field on this endpoint). Create the field with `field_type` set, then populate its options in BD admin's form editor. Until options are populated, the rendered field will be empty.

**See also:** `updateFormField` (modify existing).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for bulk operations. Changes are immediately visible on the live site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` — includes the server-assigned ID (e.g., `user_id`, `post_id`). Use this ID for follow-up operations.

**`field_type` valid values (BD admin form-builder UI, full list):**

*Select-type fields:* `Checkbox` (checkboxes), `Select` (dropdown list), `Radio` (radio buttons), `YesNo` (yes/no toggle)

*Text inputs:* `Custom` (custom HTML), `Email`, `HTML` (section title / heading), `Button` (submit button), `Textbox` (single-line text), `textarea` (paragraph), `Url` (website URL)

*Fancy fields:* `Date` (date picker), `DateTimeLocal` (date + time picker), `File` (file upload), `FroalaEditor` (rich text — basic), `FroalaEditorUserUpload` (+ image upload), `FroalaEditorUserUploadPreMadeElem` (+ pre-made elements), `FroalaEditorAdmin` (+ media manager), `Tip` (help alert box), `Hidden`, `Country` (country list), `State` (state list), `Number` (integer), `Password`, `Phone`, `CountryCodePhone`, `Pricebox`, `ReCaptcha` (Google reCAPTCHA), `HoneyPot` (spam protection), `Category` (top-level category list), `Years` (year picker)

**Field naming rules:**
- `field_name` — internal system key. **Underscores only, no spaces.** E.g. `first_name`, `company_email`. Used as HTML `name` attribute.
- `field_text` — public-facing display label shown to form users. Free-form, e.g. "First Name", "Your Email Address".

**⚠️ Required tail-end pattern for submittable forms.** When the parent form's `form_action_type` is `widget`, `notification`, or `redirect` (see `createForm`), the fields list MUST end with these three fields in this exact order — highest `field_order` values, last 3 positions:

1. `field_type=ReCaptcha` (e.g. `field_order=98`)
2. `field_type=HoneyPot` (e.g. `field_order=99`)
3. `field_type=Button` (e.g. `field_order=100`)

Without all three at the end in order, BD errors on submit and the form won't function. ReCaptcha and HoneyPot need no configuration beyond `field_type` — pick any internal `field_name` (e.g. `recaptcha_field`, `hp_field`) and any `field_text` (empty string for ReCaptcha, something like `Leave this blank` for HoneyPot). The `Button` field's `field_text` is the visible submit-button label (e.g. `Send Message`, `Submit`).

**Exactly ONE `Button` per form.** Adding multiple Buttons causes UI confusion and submission bugs.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

field_type.description:
Form field type. Authoritative list from BD admin form-field editor, grouped by category:

SELECT FIELDS (user picks from options):
  Checkbox       = Checkboxes (multi-select)
  Select         = Dropdown list (single-select)
  Radio          = Multiple choice radios (single-select)
  YesNo          = Yes or No toggle

TEXT INPUTS:
  Custom         = Custom HTML injection
  Email          = Email address input (validates format)
  HTML           = Section title / HTML heading (display-only)
  Button         = Submit form button
  Textbox        = Single-line text input
  textarea       = Multi-line textarea (note: lowercase)
  Url            = Website URL input

FANCY FIELDS:
  Date                              = Calendar date picker
  DateTimeLocal                     = Calendar date+time picker
  File                              = File upload
  FroalaEditor                      = Froala rich text editor (basic)
  FroalaEditorUserUpload            = Froala + user image upload
  FroalaEditorUserUploadPreMadeElem = Froala + upload + pre-made elements
  FroalaEditorAdmin                 = Froala with admin media manager access
  Tip                               = Help alert box (display-only)
  Hidden                            = Hidden field (not rendered, stores value)
  Country                           = Country dropdown
  State                             = State dropdown
  Number                            = Integer-only number input
  Password                          = Password field (masked)
  Phone                             = Phone number input
  CountryCodePhone                  = Phone input with country code
  Pricebox                          = Price input
  ReCaptcha                         = Google reCAPTCHA security
  HoneyPot                          = Hidden honeypot (anti-spam)
  Category                          = Top-level category list
  Years                             = Year picker

Note on casing: most values are TitleCase but "textarea" is lowercase — copy exact spelling from the enum. For most simple text collection use Textbox; for paragraphs use textarea; for emails use Email so BD validates the format.

For Checkbox / Select / Radio types, you must also supply the options list via the field_options parameter (if exposed).

Search-result display priority (integer, lower number = higher in results).
  0 = highest priority (shows at the top of public search results)

get.description:
Paginated enumeration of SUB-level member categories (services). Read-only.

Sub Categories are level 2 of the 3-tier member classification (e.g., "Sushi" under "Restaurants"). Each has a `profession_id` pointing at its parent Top Category. `master_id` points at a parent Sub Category for sub-sub-category nesting (`master_id=0` = directly under a Top Category). Backed by BD's `list_services` table.

**Use when:** enumerating sub-categories (services) — always filter by `profession_id` to scope to one Top Category, otherwise you get all sub-cats across all tops (noisy). For sub-sub nesting, `master_id` filter narrows further.

**Permission note — platform gap:** this endpoint (`/api/v2/list_services/*`) is NOT in BD's public Swagger spec, so the admin's API key permissions UI does NOT auto-generate a toggle for it. The admin's "Services" toggle gates the Swagger-documented `/api/v2/service/*` endpoints (a DIFFERENT legacy table) — enabling that toggle does NOT grant access here. On 403: admin must manually INSERT a row into `bd_api_key_permissions` for `endpoint_path='/api/v2/list_services/get'` (and the singular `/api/v2/list_services/get/{service_id}` for `getSubCategory`). Do NOT substitute `/api/v2/service/*` — different table, inconsistent data.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` token.

**Filter/sort:** filter by `profession_id` to scope to sub-categories under one top category. Standard `property`/`property_value`/`property_operator` for other fields.

**See also:** `getSubCategory` (single by ID), `listTopCategories` (parents), `createSubCategory` (add new).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", total, ..., message: [...records] }`. Each record has `service_id`, `name`, `desc`, `profession_id`, `master_id`, `filename`, `keywords`, `sort_order`, `lead_price`, `image`.


---

**Member Category Hierarchy (3 levels):**

BD classifies members through a 3-level taxonomy — AI agents MUST understand all three to correctly create, assign, and query member categories:

| Level | Tool nicknames | Endpoint | BD internal table | Key field | Parent reference |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1. Top Category** | `listTopCategories`, `createTopCategory`, etc. | `/api/v2/list_professions/*` | `list_professions` | `profession_id` (PK) | — |
| **2. Sub Category** | `listSubCategories`, `createSubCategory`, etc. | `/api/v2/list_services/*` | `list_services` | `service_id` (PK) | `profession_id` → parent Top Category; `master_id` → parent Sub Category (for sub-sub nesting; 0 = direct child of Top) |
| **3. Member ↔ Sub Category link** | `listMemberSubCategoryLinks`, `createMemberSubCategoryLink`, etc. | `/api/v2/rel_services/*` | `rel_services` | `rel_id` (PK) | `user_id` → member; `service_id` → Sub Category |

**How a member gets classified on their public profile:**
- `users_data.profession_id` → points at a single Top Category (the member's primary classification; shown in URL slug)
- `users_data.services` → CSV of Sub Category IDs the member is tagged with (multiple allowed; simpler than the join table)
- `rel_services` rows (Member ↔ Sub Category links) → used when you need per-link metadata like `avg_price`, `specialty`, `num_completed`. Optional; most sites use just the CSV field.

**Sub-sub-categories:** `createSubCategory` with `master_id=<parent service_id>` creates a Sub Category nested under another Sub Category (a "sub-sub"). `master_id=0` (default) means the Sub Category sits directly under a Top Category (the `profession_id`).

**There is NO `createProfession` or `createService` tool in this MCP** — those are the BD internal resource names. Use the tools named above.


_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

Links an existing member (`user_id`) to an existing Sub Category (`service_id`) with per-link metadata (pricing, specialty). This is the richer alternative to setting `users_data.services` CSV via `updateUser` — use this when you need per-link data.

- `specialty` — `0` or `1` flags this Sub Category as a specialty offering on the member's profile
- `num_completed` — counter of jobs/projects completed in this Sub Category
- `date` — YYYYMMDDHHmmss timestamp

**See also:** `updateUser` with `services="<csv>"` (simpler, no per-link metadata), `listSubCategories`, `getMemberSubCategoryLink`.

get.description:
Paginated enumeration of users_meta records (EAV key/value table). Read-only.

**Use when:** enumerating metadata rows (key/value pairs) attached to a parent record in any BD table.

**⚠️ IDENTITY RULE — filter by `database` AND `database_id` together, never just `database_id` alone.** The same `database_id` value can exist in users_meta pointing at different parent tables simultaneously — e.g. `database_id=123` may refer to rows in `users_data`, `list_seo`, `data_posts`, and `subscription_types` as four UNRELATED records. If you only filter by `database_id`, you'll get rows from all of them mixed together. **This is not theoretical — live-verified 2026-04-19:** filtering `listUserMeta` by `database_id=149` alone on a live test site returned 12 rows for one user — only 3 were legitimate user meta (from `database=users_data`); the other 9 were admin-session breadcrumbs from `database=data_categories` with the same numeric ID. The cross-table noise RATIO was 3:1 NOT-what-you-wanted. ALWAYS also filter by `database=<parent_table>` (e.g. `list_seo`, `users_data`) — either via array-syntax multi-filter if supported, OR by listing by `database_id` and client-side filtering the results by the `database` field before acting. This rule is critical for all downstream writes (`updateUserMeta`, `deleteUserMeta`) — misidentifying a row can silently corrupt unrelated resource metadata.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` token from the previous response's `next_page`. Do not assume integer offsets.

**Filter/sort:** `property` + `property_value` + `property_operator` (`=`, `LIKE`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`) to filter; `order_column` + `order_type` (`ASC`/`DESC`) to sort. Useful filters: `database`, `database_id`, `key`.

**See also:** `getUserMeta` (single record by ID), `updateUserMeta`, `deleteUserMeta`.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", total, current_page, total_pages, next_page, prev_page, message: [...records] }`. Each record includes `meta_id`, `database`, `database_id`, `key`, `value`, `date_added`, `revision_timestamp`.

**Note on duplicates:** BD does NOT enforce a uniqueness constraint on `(database, database_id, key)` — the same field can have multiple rows (observed live). Read-layer merge uses last-write-wins, but stored data can bloat. When updating, consider patching ALL matching rows, or deleting duplicates first.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

post.description:
Create a new users_meta record (EAV key/value attached to any BD table row). Writes live data.

**Use when:**
- Attaching an arbitrary custom key/value to a member (`database=users_data`, `database_id=<user_id>`)
- Writing to a subscription plan extension (`database=subscription_types`)
- **Critical BD pattern: updating WebPage (`list_seo`) fields that `updateWebPage` doesn't persist on the update path.** BD's `list_seo` table mixes direct columns with EAV-stored fields in `users_meta`. On UPDATE, `updateWebPage` only writes direct columns — for these 18 fields you MUST use `createUserMeta` (if the meta row doesn't exist) or `updateUserMeta` (if it does): `linked_post_category`, `linked_post_type`, `disable_preview_screenshot`, `disable_css_stylesheets`, `hero_content_overlay_opacity`, `hero_link_target_blank`, `hero_background_image_size`, `hero_link_size`, `hero_link_color`, `hero_content_font_size`, `hero_section_content`, `hero_column_width`, `h2_font_weight`, `h1_font_weight`, `h2_font_size`, `h1_font_size`, `hero_link_text`, `hero_link_url`. Pattern: `database=list_seo`, `database_id=<seo_id>`, `key=<field>`, `value=<value>`.

**Required:** `database`, `database_id`, `key`, `value`.

**Parameter interactions:**
- `database` — target BD table name (e.g. `users_data`, `subscription_types`, `list_seo`, `data_posts`)
- `database_id` — PK of the record in that table to attach metadata to
- `key` — the meta field name. Must match the spelling the rendering layer expects (e.g. `hero_content_overlay_opacity` for the hero overlay).
- `value` — stored as longtext; any string or serialized value
- `date_added` — optional, `YYYYMMDDHHmmss`

**⚠️ Duplicate `(database, database_id, key)` triple silent-accept (live-verified 2026-04-19):** BD does NOT enforce uniqueness on the natural key. Two rapid `createUserMeta` calls with the same `database`+`database_id`+`key` both succeed and produce two rows with different `meta_id`s. Subsequent `updateUserMeta` / filter-by-key reads become non-deterministic (MySQL returns whichever row it indexes first, effectively last-write-wins-but-not-really). **Always run the list-first workflow below** before create — never blindly create and assume idempotency.

**Workflow for updating a users_meta field on an existing parent record:**
1. `listUserMeta` with filter `database=<table>`, `database_id=<id>`, `key=<field>` — to check if the row already exists.
2. If row exists → `updateUserMeta(meta_id, value=<new>, database=<table>, database_id=<id>)` — all four required as of v6.7.1.
3. If row does NOT exist → `createUserMeta(database=<table>, database_id=<id>, key=<field>, value=<new>)`.

**After hero/layout EAV-field creates on a WebPage:** call `refreshSiteCache` so changes show publicly. Applies equally to creating a brand-new EAV row for a hero/layout field and to updating one.

**See also:** `updateUserMeta` (modify existing), `listUserMeta` (check for existing row first), `deleteUserMeta` (clean up orphans after parent-record deletion).

**Cleanup rule:** when an agent deletes a parent record (e.g. `deleteWebPage(seo_id=X)`), BD does NOT cascade-delete the corresponding users_meta rows. List orphans via `listUserMeta(database=list_seo, database_id=X)`, client-side filter to rows whose `database` equals `list_seo`, then call `deleteUserMeta(meta_id=..., database=list_seo, database_id=X)` for each — all three required as of v6.7.1. Surgical only — never bulk-delete across unrelated `database_id`s.

**Writes live data:** confirm intent for bulk operations.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` — includes the new `meta_id`.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

database.description: REQUIRED — parent table name this meta row is attached to (e.g. `list_seo`, `users_data`, `subscription_types`). MUST match the `database` value stored on the row being updated. This is a hard safety rule — the same `meta_id` verification plus `(database, database_id)` scoping prevents cross-table metadata corruption since the same `database_id` number can exist across multiple parent tables.

database_id.description: REQUIRED — PK of the parent record this meta row is attached to. MUST match the `database_id` value stored on the row being updated. Same safety rule as `database`: the same numeric ID can belong to unrelated rows on different tables.

put.description:
Update an existing users_meta record's `value` by `meta_id`. Fields omitted are untouched. Writes live data.

**⚠️ IDENTITY RULE — ALWAYS confirm BOTH `database` AND `database_id` before updating.** A users_meta row is identified by the PAIR `(database, database_id)` PLUS `key`. The same `database_id` value can exist pointing at different parent tables (e.g. `database_id=120` might refer to a member in `users_data` AND a page in `list_seo` AND a subscription in `subscription_types` — completely unrelated records sharing the same ID). ALWAYS verify the row's `database` field matches the table you intend to modify BEFORE calling `updateUserMeta` — misidentifying the row could silently overwrite unrelated resource metadata.

**Use when:**
- Changing a metadata value on any BD table row that was previously created via `createUserMeta`.
- **Critical BD pattern: updating WebPage (`list_seo`) fields that `updateWebPage` silently skips.** BD's `list_seo` table mixes direct columns with EAV-stored values in `users_meta`. The `updateWebPage` endpoint only writes direct columns; for these 18 fields you MUST use this endpoint: `linked_post_category`, `linked_post_type`, `disable_preview_screenshot`, `disable_css_stylesheets`, `hero_content_overlay_opacity`, `hero_link_target_blank`, `hero_background_image_size`, `hero_link_size`, `hero_link_color`, `hero_content_font_size`, `hero_section_content`, `hero_column_width`, `h2_font_weight`, `h1_font_weight`, `h2_font_size`, `h1_font_size`, `hero_link_text`, `hero_link_url`.

**Workflow:** find the `meta_id` by calling `listUserMeta` with filter `database=<table>`, `database_id=<parent_id>`, `key=<field>`. Then call this endpoint with `meta_id`, `value`, and the same `database` + `database_id` you used for lookup. If no row exists for that combo, use `createUserMeta` instead.

**Required (v6.7.1):** `meta_id`, `value`, `database`, `database_id`. All four — always. The identity pair (`database`, `database_id`) is enforced at the schema level to prevent cross-table corruption.

**After hero/layout updates on a WebPage:** call `refreshSiteCache` so changes show publicly.

**See also:** `createUserMeta` (add new when no row exists), `listUserMeta` (find the `meta_id` by filter), `deleteUserMeta` (remove permanently).

**Writes live data:** confirm intent with the user for bulk operations. Changes are immediately visible on the live site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...updatedRecord} }` — the full updated record after changes applied.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

delete.description:
Permanently delete a users_meta record by `meta_id`. DESTRUCTIVE — cannot be undone via API.

**⚠️ HARD RULE — verify BOTH `database` AND `database_id` of the row BEFORE deleting.** A users_meta row is identified by the PAIR `(database, database_id)` PLUS `key`. The same `database_id` can point at different parent tables — e.g. `database_id=123` may refer to a row in `users_data`, `list_seo`, `data_posts`, and `subscription_types` simultaneously as four UNRELATED records. BEFORE calling this endpoint: (1) call `getUserMeta(meta_id)` or keep the row's full object from a prior `listUserMeta` response; (2) confirm the row's `database` value matches the table you intend to clean up. If you're batch-deleting orphans after a parent delete: list by the parent's `database_id`, then CLIENT-SIDE filter to ONLY the rows where `database` equals the parent table's name BEFORE deleting any `meta_id`. NEVER loop-delete by `database_id` alone — you will destroy unrelated resource metadata (member data, plan metadata, page settings) that happen to share the same numeric ID on other tables.

**Use when:** removing a specific metadata row, OR cleaning up orphan meta rows after a parent record is deleted (BD does not cascade-delete users_meta when a parent is removed — it's the agent's job to find and delete the orphan rows surgically).

**Required (v6.7.1):** `meta_id`, `database`, `database_id`. All three — always. The identity pair (`database`, `database_id`) is enforced at the schema level to prevent cross-table destruction.

**Post-parent-delete cleanup workflow (safe pattern):**
1. `listUserMeta` with filter `database_id=<deleted parent's id>`
2. In the returned array, filter CLIENT-SIDE to ONLY rows where `database` equals the parent table's name (e.g. `list_seo` for a deleted WebPage)
3. For each filtered `meta_id`, call `deleteUserMeta(meta_id, database=<parent table>, database_id=<parent id>)` — all three required as of v6.7.1
4. Never skip step 2 — the same `database_id` can belong to unrelated rows on other tables

**See also:** `updateUserMeta` (modify without removing), `listUserMeta` (enumerate with filter).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: "users_meta record was deleted" }`. No body beyond the confirmation string.

**Use when:** attaching an existing tag to a record (member, post, etc.). The alternative for members is setting the `member_tags` field via `updateUser` with `member_tag_action=1`.

**Required:** `tag_id`, `object_id`, `tag_type_id`, `added_by`.

- Attaches an existing tag to an existing record. Both sides must exist — use `listTags` and the target resource's list to discover IDs

The `tag_type_id` determines WHICH resource/table the `object_id` lives in. Discover mapping via `listTagTypes` — each tag type row has a `table_relation` field naming its target table. Example mapping:

| tag_type_id | type_name | Target table (table_relation) | What object_id references |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Users | `users_data` | `user_id` |
| (other rows) | (other types) | e.g. `data_widgets`, `menus`, `forms` | That table's primary key |

**Process:** call `listTagTypes` first to see the `tag_type_id` → `table_relation` mapping on your site, then pick the appropriate `tag_type_id` and supply the matching record's PK as `object_id`. Widgets, menus, and forms all support tags via this same mechanism.

- For `smart_list_type=newsletter`: store a URL string (admin view uses it directly as an href link — no filter semantics).

Paginated enumeration of CUSTOM sidebars defined on this site. Read-only in this MCP (create/update/delete deliberately omitted — sidebars are layout infrastructure; changes belong in the BD admin UI).

**Use when:** an agent needs to set `form_name` on a WebPage (the sidebar assignment) and wants to verify a custom sidebar name exists on this site before using it.

**Important — this endpoint returns ONLY custom sidebars.** It does NOT return the 6 **Master Default Sidebars** that are seeded in BD's master database and always valid on every site. Those are hardcoded in BD core and are NOT rows in the `sidebars` table. Always-available master defaults (use these names verbatim in `form_name`):
- `Global Website Search`
- `Member Profile Page`
- `Member Search Result`
- `Personal Post Feed`
- `Post Search Result`
- `Post Single Page`

**Agent workflow when user names a sidebar:** (1) check the 6 master defaults first (string match, exact case). (2) If not a master, call `listSidebars` and look for a row whose `name` matches. (3) If neither match, the sidebar does NOT exist — ASK THE USER to pick from the master defaults or an available custom sidebar (do NOT force an unknown name into `form_name` — BD will render no sidebar).

**Returns:** rows with `sidebar_id`, `name` (display name — this is the VALUE to pass to `form_name`), `desc`, `active` (`1`/`0`), `separator`, `css`, `script`, `short_code`, `type`, `div_id`, `div_class`, `revision_timestamp`.

**Pagination + filter/sort:** standard.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header._

**Only returns custom sidebars.** The 6 master default sidebars (`Global Website Search`, `Member Profile Page`, `Member Search Result`, `Personal Post Feed`, `Post Search Result`, `Post Single Page`) are not rows — use those names directly in `form_name` without looking them up.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: [{...record}] }`.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header._

Paginated enumeration of cities enabled on this site for location-based member/post browsing and SEO page URL generation. Read-only source-of-truth for city slugs used in search-result URLs. Backed by BD's `location_cities` table.

**Use when:** resolving a human city name (e.g. "Beverly Hills") to its `city_filename` slug (e.g. `beverly-hills`) before constructing a search-result URL for a static SEO page, or discovering which cities this site has seeded.

**Pagination:** cursor-based (`limit`, `page`).

**Filter/sort:** standard `property`/`property_value`/`property_operator`. Useful filters: `city_ln` (full name, exact match), `city_filename` (slug, exact), `state_sn` (scope to one state), `country_sn` (scope to one country).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: [...rows] }` — each row has:
- `locaiton_id` (integer PK, **BD schema typo — it is `locaiton_id`, NOT `location_id`**; pass the typo'd form when looking up a single record)
- `city_ln` (full name)
- `city_filename` (URL slug)
- `state_sn` (2-letter state/province code; references `location_states.state_sn`)
- `country_sn` (2-letter country code; references `list_countries.country_code`)

**System-critical table — create & delete deliberately omitted from this MCP.** Cities are managed by BD automatically (a new city row is added when a member signs up from a new location). Creating cities via API risks slug collisions with auto-created rows, and deleting risks orphaning members whose `city` references the row. Use `updateCity` only for corrections (rename, fix typo in filename). For new cities, let the next member signup seed it.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` — empty-result responses return `{status: "error", message: "location_cities not found", total: 0}` (same ambiguous pattern as other list endpoints)._
