post.description:
Create a new web page on the site. Writes live data.

A WebPage is any static-ish page: the homepage (`seo_type=home`), about, contact, custom landing pages, category templates, profile templates, and search result templates. Backed by BD's `list_seo` table.

Pages are publicly accessible **immediately** once `content_active=1` (the default) and a valid `seo_type` is supplied — no admin action needed.

**Use when:** adding a new custom landing page, about, contact, or other static-ish page. Required: `seo_type` (must match one of BD's admin-UI values — `content` is the safe default for generic pages) and `filename` (URL slug). Pages are live immediately once `content_active=1` (default).

**Required:** `seo_type`, `filename`.

**⚠️ Asset routing quick-reference — which field does my code go in?**

- **Body HTML** → `content`. Froala editor; strips `<style>` and `<script>` tags on save (CSS/JS in `content` is silently removed).
- **CSS rules** → `content_css`. NO `<style>` wrapper — paste raw rules. Scope selectors to a unique page class; never target `.container` / `.froala-table` / `.image-placeholder` (reserved BD utilities).
- **JavaScript** → `content_footer_html`. YES — include `<script>` tags. Also pixels/analytics embeds. IIFE-wrap and scope selectors to a unique page class.
- **`<head>` dependencies** → `content_head`. Include full tags (`<link>`, `<meta>`, JSON-LD, head-required scripts).
- **Hero banner** → `enable_hero_section` + `hero_*` + `h1_*`/`h2_*`. Most hero fields are EAV-stored; on UPDATE use `updateUserMeta`/`createUserMeta` for the 18 EAV fields listed further below.
- **Page-access gate** → `content_footer`. Misleading name — NOT HTML. Enum: empty string (public) / `members_only` / `digital_products`.

**Parameter guidance:**
- `seo_type` — page type identifier. Must be one of the fixed admin-UI values (invalid types produce unrenderable records). Common values: `home` (homepage replacement), `content` (generic static/landing page — BD admin default for "new page"), profile/search template types, etc. For a generic landing/static page, use `content`.
- `filename` — URL slug (e.g. `about-us`), not the full URL. The public URL is `<site-domain>/<filename>`.
- `content` — main HTML body. Supports `[widget=Name]` shortcodes AND template tokens.
- `content_active=1` (default) — page is live; `0` = hidden.

**See also:** `listWebPages` (enumerate), `updateWebPage` (modify), `createRedirect` (if replacing an existing URL, create a 301 to preserve SEO).

**Writes live data:** changes are immediately visible on the public site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` including `seo_id`.


**BD Admin UI — field label mapping:**

Some field names are opaque; this is what they're called in the BD admin page-settings screens.

*Display/layout toggles (integer 0/1 unless noted):*
| Field | Admin UI label |
|---|---|
| `content_layout` | Full Screen Page Width |
| `section` | Hide Banner Ad Modules |
| `hide_header` | Hide Header |
| `hide_footer` | Hide Footer |
| `hide_top_right` | Hide Top Header Menu |
| `hide_header_links` | Hide Main Menu |
| `content_images` | Click-Enlarge Images (value: `click_to_enlarge` to enable) |
| `show_form` | **Apply NoIndex, NoFollow** (NOT a contact-form toggle — BD repurposed this column) |
| `disable_css_stylesheets` | Disable Default CSS Stylesheets |
| `disable_preview_screenshot` | Disable Screenshot Preview |
| `seo_text` | Wildcard URL Rewrite (catch-all routing) |

*SEO / meta:*
| Field | Admin UI label |
|---|---|
| `title` | Page Meta Title |
| `meta_desc` | Meta Description |
| `meta_keywords` | Meta Keywords |
| `h1` | H1 Heading |
| `h2` | H2 Heading |

*Social (Open Graph):*
| Field | Admin UI label |
|---|---|
| `facebook_title` | Social Media Title |
| `facebook_desc` | Social Media Description |
| `facebook_image` | Social Media Shared Image (≥200×200px) |

Template tokens: `title`, `meta_desc`, `meta_keywords`, `h1`, `h2` all support `%%%website_name%%%`, `%industry%`, `%profession%` (expanded at render time).

**Hero section (optional banner rendered above page content):** toggled via `enable_hero_section` (`0`=off, `1`=all devices, `2`=hide on mobile). The full hero config spans ~23 fields: `hero_image` (external URLs supported natively — no `auto_image_import` needed), `hero_background_image_size`, `hero_content_overlay_color` (RGB format ONLY, e.g. `rgb(0,0,0)` — hex not accepted), `hero_content_overlay_opacity`, `hero_top_padding`/`hero_bottom_padding`, `hero_column_width`, `hero_alignment`, H1 styling (`h1_font_color`/`h1_font_size`/`h1_font_weight`), H2 styling (`h2_font_color`/`h2_font_size`/`h2_font_weight`), additional content styling (`hero_content_font_color`/`hero_content_font_size`/`hero_section_content`), CTA (`hero_link_url`/`hero_link_text`/`hero_link_target_blank`/`hero_link_size`/`hero_link_color`), and `hero_hide_banner_ad` to suppress the site-wide Below Header Banner Ad on this page. All color fields require RGB format. `enable_hero_section=0` is a master kill-switch — other hero_* values remain stored but are not rendered. The H1/H2 TEXT itself comes from the page's top-level `h1` and `h2` fields; the `h1_*`/`h2_*` here control ONLY hero styling. **Homepage:** the homepage record (`seo_type=home`, typically `seo_id=1`) cannot be CREATED via API — it's system-seeded. For homepage edits use `updateWebPage` with the existing `seo_id`. **Homepage hero fields are BENIGN — stored but not rendered by the homepage template.** BD accepts and persists `enable_hero_section` + any `hero_*`/`h1_*`/`h2_*` values on the homepage record (live-verified 2026-04-19: `seo_id=1` stored `enable_hero_section=1` + `hero_image` + `hero_section_content` cleanly), but the homepage template does not render a hero banner — homepage layout is driven by its own widget/section configuration, not list_seo's hero columns. Writing hero fields to the homepage does no harm (no errors, no visible effect) but also does no good. **Recommendation:** skip hero fields when updating `seo_id=1` unless the user explicitly requests it for data-hygiene reasons. To display a hero on the homepage, use the homepage widget/section configuration instead (not scoped by this API).

**Hero safe-defaults — ALWAYS apply when `enable_hero_section=1` or `2` and the agent is the one enabling the hero** (unless the user explicitly overrides a value). These defaults ensure text is readable over any background image:
- `h1_font_color="rgb(255,255,255)"` — white H1 readable over most hero images
- `h2_font_color="rgb(255,255,255)"` — matching white H2
- `hero_content_overlay_color="rgb(0,0,0)"` — black overlay layer
- `hero_content_overlay_opacity="0.5"` — at 0.5 (admin-UI level 5 of 10), dims the background enough for white text to pop without obscuring the image. **EAV-STORED — `createWebPage` writes this correctly, but `updateWebPage` silently drops it. On updates, write it via `updateUserMeta`/`createUserMeta` with `database=list_seo`. See the EAV workaround section below.**
- `hero_top_padding="100"` — 100px breathing room above hero content
- `hero_bottom_padding="100"` — 100px below

Applies to BOTH `seo_type=content` and `seo_type=profile_search_results` pages. Universal readability rule.

**Cache refresh REQUIRED after any hero create/update:** Hero changes do NOT reflect on the public URL until the site cache is refreshed. After ANY `createWebPage` or `updateWebPage` that touches hero fields (`enable_hero_section`, any `hero_*`, `h1_font_*`, `h2_font_*`), call `refreshSiteCache` immediately. Without the refresh, the admin dashboard will show the changes but visitors will see stale hero markup until BD's next scheduled cache cycle.

**users_meta update workaround — CRITICAL for updates.** BD's `list_seo` table mixes direct columns with EAV-stored values in `users_meta`. On CREATE, `createWebPage` writes all fields correctly. On UPDATE, `updateWebPage` ONLY writes the direct columns — these 18 fields get silently ignored by the update path and must be written via `updateUserMeta` / `createUserMeta` instead: `linked_post_category`, `linked_post_type`, `disable_preview_screenshot`, `disable_css_stylesheets`, `hero_content_overlay_opacity`, `hero_link_target_blank`, `hero_background_image_size`, `hero_link_size`, `hero_link_color`, `hero_content_font_size`, `hero_section_content`, `hero_column_width`, `h2_font_weight`, `h1_font_weight`, `h2_font_size`, `h1_font_size`, `hero_link_text`, `hero_link_url`. Workflow per field: (1) `listUserMeta` filter by `database=list_seo`, `database_id=<seo_id>`, `key=<field>` to find the existing `meta_id`; (2) exists → `updateUserMeta(meta_id, value=...)`; (3) missing → `createUserMeta(database=list_seo, database_id=<seo_id>, key=<field>, value=<new value>)`. Reads merge automatically via `getWebPage`/`listWebPages` — you only need the workaround for WRITES. On `deleteWebPage`, also delete the corresponding users_meta rows (filter by the deleted `seo_id`) to prevent orphans.

**Hero image sourcing for agents:** never use random-image placeholders (picsum.photos, lorempixel, placekitten) — those change per page load and look broken to real visitors. Pick a content-relevant stock photo from Pexels (free license, no attribution required) via a search term derived from the page topic. Use the LARGE variant URL (Pexels provides `?auto=compress&cs=tinysrgb&w=1800` as a query string on the images hostname) — not `original`, which is often 5000+px and too heavy for a hero banner. If the user supplies their own image URL, use that.

**Member Search Results SEO pages (`seo_type=profile_search_results`) — thin-content remedy workflow:** BD auto-generates dynamic search-result pages for every valid combination of location + category on the site (e.g. `california/beverly-hills/plumbers`). Google often penalizes these when they return only 1–2 members (thin content). The remedy is to create a STATIC `list_seo` record with `seo_type=profile_search_results`, `filename=<the exact URL slug>`, and `content=<unique SEO copy>` (intro paragraph, local context, FAQ, etc.) — that static record then overrides the dynamic rendering and the custom copy renders alongside the live results.

**CRITICAL — `filename` MUST be a real location/category slug BD's dynamic router recognizes.** The `profile_search_results` static page works by OVERRIDING an existing dynamic search URL. If the `filename` does NOT correspond to a valid BD dynamic URL built from actual country/state/city/top_category/sub_category slugs on THIS site, **the public URL will return HTTP 404 even though the `list_seo` record exists with `content_active=1`**. BD has no dynamic page to override, so nothing renders. Arbitrary / made-up / pattern-generator / test slugs like `foo-bar-baz`, `my-cool-page`, or `hero-test-page` WILL NOT render as `profile_search_results` pages — live-verified on 2026-04-19. Every segment of the slug must come from a verified live lookup: 
  - country segment (optional left-parent): `listCountries` → lowercase `country_name` with spaces → hyphens
  - state segment: `listStates` → `state_filename`
  - city segment: `listCities` → `city_filename`
  - top-category segment: `listTopCategories` → `filename`
  - sub-category segment: `listSubCategories` → `filename`
Do NOT construct `profile_search_results` slugs from arbitrary strings. If the user wants a static page at an arbitrary URL, use `seo_type=content` instead (content pages create their own route and render at any `filename`).

*URL slug construction (the `filename` field, NO leading slash):* segments use hierarchy order and any left-parent is droppable to broaden the match. Always concatenate with `/` separators:
  - Fullest: `country/state/city/top_cat/sub_cat`
  - Common: `state/city/top_cat/sub_cat` (when site is single-country)
  - `city/top_cat/sub_cat`, `top_cat/sub_cat`, `city`, `state`, `top_cat`, etc.

*Where each segment comes from:*
  - `country` segment: NOT a stored slug — derive from `list_countries.country_name` (via `listCountries`) by lowercasing and replacing spaces with hyphens (e.g. `"United States"` → `united-states`)
  - `state` segment: `location_states.state_filename` (via `listStates`)
  - `city` segment: `location_cities.city_filename` (via `listCities`)
  - `top_cat` segment: top category's `filename` (via `listTopCategories`)
  - `sub_cat` segment: sub-category's `filename` (via `listSubCategories`)

*Agent workflow for "add SEO to [category] in [location] page":*
  1. Resolve each named input to its slug via the relevant list endpoint (`listCities`, `listStates`, `listTopCategories`, `listSubCategories`) filtering by the full/human name field — exact-match `=` is reliable for these.
  2. For ambiguous requests (e.g. user says "Beverly Hills plumbers" — could be `beverly-hills/plumbers` OR `california/beverly-hills/plumbers`), ask the user which URL variant they want UNLESS they've explicitly requested bulk/pattern-based updates.
  3. Check for an existing `list_seo` record at that slug: `listWebPages` with `property=filename&property_value=<slug>&property_operator==`. If found → `updateWebPage`. If not → `createWebPage` with `seo_type=profile_search_results`, `filename=<slug>`, `content=<SEO HTML>`. **Always run the check-first step — BD does NOT reject duplicate `filename` values. If you skip the check and the page already exists, BD silently creates a second row with its own `seo_id`; both records will exist, and which one wins at render time is undefined. Duplicates orphan silently, no warning.**
  4. On create, **required defaults** for `profile_search_results` pages (unless user explicitly overrides):
     - `seo_type=profile_search_results`
     - `content_active=1`
     - `custom_html_placement=4` (Below Body Content — safest for boilerplate SEO intro without disrupting the live member UI)
     - `form_name="Member Profile Page"` (sidebar — one of the one of the 6 Master Default Sidebars; always available on every site)
     - `menu_layout=3` (Left Slim — sidebar position)
     - `date_updated=<current YYYYMMDDHHmmss>` — set on EVERY create and update (BD does not auto-populate; blank leaves admin UI showing no update date)
     - `updated_by` — optional but recommended, e.g. `"AI Agent"` or `"API"` for audit trail
     - **If user requests a different sidebar and it is NOT in the 6 master defaults OR `listSidebars` customs → ask:** "That sidebar isn't available on this site. OK to use `Member Profile Page` (default) instead, or would you like to pick from [list of available]?"
  5. **MUST auto-generate SEO-optimized meta for the specific location+category combo** — don't leave these blank on a search-results SEO page, that defeats the whole purpose. Agent populates the following from the human names (not slugs), using natural "in [city]" / "[city] [category] near me" / "best [category] in [location]" phrasing, respecting each field's char budget, and VARYING Title vs Social Title so social platforms show different copy than the Google snippet:
     - `title` — Page Meta Title. 50–60 chars ideal, ≤70 hard max. Format: `"[Top Sub-category or Top category] in [City], [State] | [Site Name]"` or similar. BD stores the value verbatim — Google/Bing truncate at ~55–60 chars in the SERP snippet, so keep the important words in the first 55. Pre-truncate client-side if you want precise control.
     - `meta_desc` — Meta Description. 150–160 chars ideal, ≤170 max. 1–2 sentence pitch naming the location and category, with a CTA.
     - `meta_keywords` — comma-separated, ~200 chars. Low SEO weight today but fill anyway: `"[category] [city], [sub-cat] [city], [category] near me, [city] [state] [category]"`.
     - `facebook_title` — Open Graph title. 55–60 chars ideal. Should differ from `title` — more conversational for social shares.
     - `facebook_desc` — Open Graph description. 110–125 chars ideal. Shorter than `meta_desc`, punchier for social feeds.
     - **DO NOT auto-set `facebook_image`** — requires an uploaded asset from the customer.
  6. **h1/h2 double-render trap:** if the hero section is enabled (`enable_hero_section=1` or `2`), the page's `h1` and `h2` text fields render inside the hero banner. If your `content` HTML ALSO contains an `<h1>` or `<h2>`, the page renders BOTH — creating duplicate headings (bad for SEO). Safe rule: if you put `<h1>`/`<h2>` inside `content`, leave the page's `h1`/`h2` fields empty; if you want the hero to show the heading, set `h1`/`h2` on the record and don't repeat it in `content`.
  7. `custom_html_placement` is only meaningful on `profile_search_results` (and later `data_category`) page types — ignored on regular `content` pages.
  8. **Country-only slug caveat (live-verified):** `filename=<country_slug>` (e.g. `united-states` by itself) stores as a `list_seo` record but **does NOT render a public URL** — BD has no standalone country-level dynamic search route, so the slug 404s. The country slug is only useful as a LEFT-PARENT PREFIX to a longer slug (e.g. `united-states/california/los-angeles/plumbers`). Country-alone works fine for `content`-type pages via `filename`; the 404 is specific to `profile_search_results`.

BD handles the dynamic-to-static takeover automatically: once a `list_seo` record exists with a matching slug and `content_active=1`, it overrides the dynamic page for that URL. Millions of dynamic search URLs can exist on a site without any `list_seo` rows — they only enter `list_seo` when an admin/API explicitly converts them to static pages.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

seo_type.description:
Page type identifier. These are the values BD customers can choose from in admin:
  content                  = Single Web Page (USE THIS for custom/landing/static pages — it is the default)
  data_category            = Post Search Results
  profile_search_results   = Member Search Results
  custom_widget_page       = Custom Widget as Web Page
  password_retrieval_page  = Password Retrieval Page
  unsubscribed             = Unsubscribed Page
When a user asks for a "landing page", "static page", "about page", "contact page", or any generic custom page, always use "content".
Note: the underlying BD system has additional internal seo_type values (home, profile, payment, etc.) that are system-seeded and should NOT be created via API — customers never have a reason to call createPage with those.

nickname.description: Human-readable label shown in admin panel

title.description: **Page Meta Title** — Supports template tokens: `%%%website_name%%%`, `%industry%`, `%profession%`, etc. ~30-60 chars recommended. HTML title tag — supports template tokens like %%%website_name%%%

meta_keywords.description: **Meta Keywords** — Supports template tokens (comma-separated). Meta keywords — supports template tokens

meta_desc.description: **Meta Description** — Supports template tokens. ~150-160 chars recommended for search snippets. Meta description — supports template tokens

seo_text.description: **Wildcard URL Rewrite** — 1 = any URL within this directory routes to this web page (catch-all behavior). Pass raw HTML — do NOT wrap in `<![CDATA[...]]>`, do NOT escape as `<` / `>`. BD stores the field value verbatim; any wrapper/escape gets saved as literal text.

h1.description: **H1 Heading** — Supports template tokens. Rendered as the page's main heading. H1 heading — supports template tokens

h2.description: **H2 Heading** — Supports template tokens. H2 heading — supports template tokens

content.description: Main page body — rendered by a Froala rich text editor. **HTML only, no `<style>` or `<script>` tags** (Froala strips them). Also strips `<form>`, `<input>`, `<select>`, `<textarea>`, and `contenteditable` attributes. **Do NOT put CSS or JS here** — route them to the dedicated fields: CSS → `content_css`, JS/scripts → `content_footer_html`, head-only dependencies → `content_head`. For one-off styling, inline `style="..."` attributes on elements work fine. Supports `[widget=Name]` / `[form=Name]` shortcodes and `%%%template_tokens%%%`. Pass raw HTML — do NOT wrap in `<![CDATA[...]]>`, do NOT escape as `<` / `>`. BD stores the value verbatim.

content_css.description: Custom CSS for this page. **Paste raw CSS rules directly — do NOT wrap in `<style>` tags.** Renders in the page `<head>` at load time. **Scope every selector** under a unique page-specific class or ID (e.g. `.my-about-page h2 { ... }`) — unscoped selectors like `body`, `h1`, `p` affect the entire site. Never target reserved platform classes: `.container`, `.froala-table`, `.image-placeholder` — these are BD utilities, not styling targets.

content_footer_html.description: Page-scoped **JavaScript and other scripts** — rendered just before the closing `</body>` tag. Wrap JS in `<script>` tags here (unlike `content`, this field accepts them). jQuery is already loaded globally on BD sites. Best practice: wrap custom JS in an IIFE `(function($){ /* ... */ })(jQuery);` and scope all selectors to a unique page class so JS doesn't leak to other pages. Also the right place for third-party script embeds scoped to this page only (analytics pixels, chat widgets, schema, etc.). **Not for extra body HTML** — `content` is the body. This field is strictly for script / dependency tags.

content_head.description: Page-scoped `<head>` dependencies — rendered inside the `<head>` tag. Use for `<link>` tags (external stylesheets, preconnect hints, canonical overrides), `<meta>` tags beyond what the standard SEO fields cover, verification tags, structured-data `<script type="application/ld+json">`, and third-party `<script>` tags that MUST load in the head (rare — prefer `content_footer_html` for most JS).

content_footer.description:
⚠️ **MISLEADING NAME — this is NOT page footer HTML.** Misnamed relic column; BD repurposed it as the **page-access gate**. Controls who can view the rendered public URL:
- `""` (empty, default) — **Public For Everyone**
- `"members_only"` — **Only Allow Members** (logged-in members only; non-members hit a login/signup wall)
- `"digital_products"` — **Only Allow Digital Products Buyers** (only buyers of digital-product items on the site)

Finer-grained rules (which members, which plans, etc.) are handled by other related fields BD exposes separately; this column is just the top-level gate. Do NOT put HTML in this field — for page body use `content`, for scripts/JS use `content_footer_html`, for below-body HTML use `content` (there's no separate 'extra HTML below body' field).

content_menu.description: Menu section this page belongs to

content_layout.description: **Full Screen Page Width** — layout flag that enables edge-to-edge sections on this page. `0` (default) = content stays inside the site's normal max-width container (the contained layout used for most static pages). `1` = full-screen width, so individual rows/sections inside `content` can break out to the browser edges (useful for full-bleed background colors, hero-style bands, or image backgrounds that need to span the viewport). Only set to `1` if the page's HTML has sections designed to go edge-to-edge; plain text/content pages don't need it.

menu_layout.description:
Sidebar position and width (integer). Only has an effect when the page has a sidebar configured via `form_name` — ignored on pages without a sidebar.
  1 = Left Wide    (sidebar on the left, wide column) — BD default when unspecified
  2 = Right Wide   (sidebar on the right, wide column)
  3 = Left Slim    (sidebar on the left, narrow column)
  4 = Right Slim   (sidebar on the right, narrow column)
Note the ordering is NOT sequential by side — left positions are 1 and 3, right positions are 2 and 4. Despite the field name, this is the SIDEBAR position setting, not a navigation menu layout.
**Default on create for `profile_search_results` pages:** `3` (Left Slim). For regular `content` pages, omit unless user specifies — BD defaults to 1 (Left Wide).

show_form.description: **Apply NoIndex, NoFollow** — 1 = adds `<meta name="robots" content="noindex,nofollow">` to the page. Auto-applied to protected pages. NOT a contact-form toggle (despite the field name — BD repurposed this column).

form_name.description: **SIDEBAR name** for this page — BD's field is misnamed (it controls the sidebar slot, NOT a contact form). Pass the exact sidebar `name` string. Empty string `""` = no sidebar. Five **Master Default Sidebars** are always available on every site (not returned by `listSidebars` — hardcoded in BD core): `Global Website Search`, `Member Profile Page`, `Personal Post Feed`, `Post Search Result`, `Post Single Page`. Custom sidebars are discoverable via `listSidebars` (returns only site-local customs). If a user names a sidebar that's in NEITHER list → ask them to pick from what's available; don't send an unknown name (BD renders no sidebar). When `form_name` is set, `menu_layout` controls position. **Default on create for `profile_search_results` pages:** `Member Profile Page`.

hide_header_links.description: **Hide Main Menu** — 1 = hides the main navigation menu on this page.

hide_header.description: **Hide Header** — 1 = hides the full site header on this page.

hide_footer.description: **Hide Footer** — 1 = hides the site footer on this page.

hide_top_right.description: **Hide Top Header Menu** — 1 = hides the top-right nav cluster (account/login links).

facebook_title.description: **Social Media Title (Open Graph)** — Title shown when the page is shared on Facebook/LinkedIn/etc. Open Graph title for social sharing

facebook_desc.description: **Social Media Description (Open Graph)** — Description shown on social shares. Open Graph description

facebook_image.description: **Social Media Shared Image** — URL/filename of the OG image. BD recommends at least 200×200px. Open Graph image URL

date_updated.description: Last-update timestamp on the page record (drives the admin-UI "Last Update" display). Format: `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` (14-digit, no separators — e.g. `20260214232533`). **Required on every `createWebPage` and `updateWebPage` call** — always set to the CURRENT timestamp at call time (unless backfilling historical data from a migration, in which case pass the historical value). BD does NOT auto-populate this field; if you omit it, the admin UI shows a blank/stale date.

updated_by.description: Label shown in admin-UI "Updated By" column on the page record. Free-form string (e.g. `"API"`, `"AI Agent"`, an admin username). Recommended on create/update for audit traceability so admins can tell which edits came from the API vs. the UI.

custom_html_placement.description:
Where to render the page's custom SEO content (`content` HTML) in relation to the dynamic search results, on `seo_type=profile_search_results` pages. Only meaningful for search-result static pages (and later `seo_type=data_category`) — ignored on regular `content` pages.
  0 = Inside Tab                (content + member results render in separate nav tabs)
  1 = Above Member Results      (within the results container; respects sidebar width)
  2 = Below Member Results      (within the results container)
  3 = Above Body Content        (full page container width — spans above sidebar + results)
  4 = Below Body Content        (full page container width — below sidebar + results) ← **recommended default for AI-generated SEO pages**
For boilerplate SEO intro paragraphs / FAQ / local copy aimed at bolstering thin pages, `4` is the safest default because it renders full-width below the live results without disrupting the primary member-facing UX.

enable_hero_section.description: Master switch for the page's hero banner (the full-width header section rendered ABOVE the main page content). `0` = disabled (no hero renders, regardless of other hero_* values), `1` = enabled on all devices, `2` = enabled on desktop but hidden on mobile. When `0`, all other `hero_*` / `h1_font_*` / `h2_font_*` fields are ignored at render time but their stored values are preserved — toggling back to `1` or `2` restores the previous config without having to re-enter it. Does NOT apply to the homepage (`seo_type=home`) — the homepage template ignores hero fields entirely. BD stores the values but never renders them on the homepage; skip all `hero_*` fields when updating seo_type=home.

hero_hide_banner_ad.description: When `1`, suppresses the site-wide "Below Header Banner Ad" on THIS page only (useful when the hero visually replaces that slot). `0` (default) keeps the banner ad in its normal position.

hero_image.description: Hero background image. Accepts either a BD-hosted relative path (e.g. `/images/bg202.webp`, produced when uploaded via the BD admin file picker) OR an external URL (e.g. `https://cdn.example.com/banner.jpg`) — external URLs are supported natively, no `auto_image_import` needed for web pages. Recommended dimensions: 1800 × 600 px.

hero_background_image_size.description: Controls how the hero background image scales/crops across devices. `mobile-ready` (recommended) = responsive behavior tuned for mobile, `standard` = fixed-ratio behavior.

hero_content_overlay_color.description: Semi-transparent color layer rendered between the hero background image and the text content, used to improve text legibility over busy images. RGB format ONLY — e.g. `rgb(0,0,0)` or `rgb(255,255,255)`. Hex (`#000000`) is NOT accepted. Combine with `hero_content_overlay_opacity` to control visibility.

hero_content_overlay_opacity.description: Opacity of the `hero_content_overlay_color` layer, in 0.1 increments from `0.0` (fully transparent / invisible) to `1` (fully opaque). Admin UI labels these as 0–10 (10 = fully opaque). Default in admin-UI creation is `0.4`. **EAV-STORED FIELD (one of the 18 list_seo fields stored in `users_meta`, not as a direct column):** `createWebPage` seeds this correctly, but `updateWebPage` silently ignores it. To update on an existing page, call `updateUserMeta` (or `createUserMeta` if no row exists yet) with `database=list_seo`, `database_id=<seo_id>`, `key=hero_content_overlay_opacity`, `value=<new value>`, then `refreshSiteCache` to re-render. Per v6.7.1 safety rule, every `updateUserMeta`/`deleteUserMeta` call must also carry `database` + `database_id`.

hero_top_padding.description: Top padding inside the hero banner, in pixels. Accepts multiples of 10 from `0` to `200` (i.e. `0`, `10`, `20`, ..., `200`). Default `70`.

hero_bottom_padding.description: Bottom padding inside the hero banner, in pixels. Accepts multiples of 10 from `0` to `200`. Default `60`.

hero_column_width.description: Width of the hero text-content column, expressed as a Bootstrap 12-col span. `3`=25%, `4`=30%, `5`=40%, `6`=50%, `7`=60%, `8`=70% (default), `9`=75%, `10`=80%, `11`=90%, `12`=100%. Narrower = more padding on the sides of the text block.

hero_alignment.description: Horizontal alignment of the hero title/subtitle/content text block within its column. Default `center`.

h1_font_color.description: Main title (H1) font color in the hero. RGB format ONLY — e.g. `rgb(255,255,255)`. The H1 text itself comes from the page's `h1` field — these `h1_*` fields control ONLY the hero's H1 styling.

h1_font_size.description: Main title (H1) font size in pixels. Accepts integer values from `30` to `80`. Default `30`.

h2_font_color.description: Sub-title (H2) font color in the hero. RGB format ONLY — e.g. `rgb(255,255,255)`. The H2 text itself comes from the page's `h2` field.

h2_font_size.description: Sub-title (H2) font size in pixels. Accepts integer values from `20` to `60`. Default `56`.

hero_content_font_color.description: Font color for the additional hero content block rendered below H1/H2 (the `hero_section_content` field). RGB format ONLY — e.g. `rgb(0,0,0)`.

hero_content_font_size.description: Font size in pixels for the additional hero content block (`hero_section_content`). Accepts integer values from `10` to `30`. Default `10`.

hero_section_content.description: Additional text/HTML/widget shortcode rendered BELOW the H1 and H2 in the hero section. Pass raw HTML — do NOT wrap in `<![CDATA[...]]>`, do NOT escape as `<` / `>`. BD stores the field value verbatim; any wrapper/escape gets saved as literal text.

hero_link_url.description: Hero call-to-action (CTA) button link URL. If empty, no CTA button is rendered. For internal links, a relative path is fine (e.g. `/signup`); for external, full URL with `http://` or `https://`.

hero_link_text.description: Hero CTA button label. Required (non-empty) for the button to render — `hero_link_url` alone without text will not produce a button.

hero_link_target_blank.description: When `1`, opens the CTA link in a new tab (`target="_blank"`). `0` (default) opens in the same tab.

hero_link_size.description: CTA button size. `""` (empty string, default) = Normal, `btn-lg` = Large, `btn-xl` = Extra Large. These are Bootstrap button-size classes applied verbatim.

hero_link_color.description: CTA button color variant. Maps to Bootstrap button classes: `primary` (default theme color), `info` (blue), `success` (green), `warning` (yellow), `danger` (red), `default` (neutral gray), `secondary` (theme secondary). Colors render per the site's theme palette, not literal colors.

page_render_widget.description: Widget ID to render as page content

seo_id.description: Page primary key (required to identify record)

seo_type.description:
Page type identifier. On update, safest to OMIT this field unless intentionally changing it (most seo_type changes are destructive). Values:
  content                  = Single Web Page (generic static/landing page)
  data_category            = Post Search Results
  profile_search_results   = Member Search Results
  custom_widget_page       = Custom Widget as Web Page
  password_retrieval_page  = Password Retrieval Page
  unsubscribed             = Unsubscribed Page
  home                     = Homepage (system-seeded; included here so an update round-trip of the existing homepage record passes the enum check)

nickname.description: Human-readable label shown in admin panel

title.description: **Page Meta Title** — Supports template tokens: `%%%website_name%%%`, `%industry%`, `%profession%`, etc. ~30-60 chars recommended. HTML title tag — supports template tokens like %%%website_name%%%

meta_keywords.description: **Meta Keywords** — Supports template tokens (comma-separated). Meta keywords — supports template tokens

meta_desc.description: **Meta Description** — Supports template tokens. ~150-160 chars recommended for search snippets. Meta description — supports template tokens

seo_text.description: **Wildcard URL Rewrite** — 1 = any URL within this directory routes to this web page (catch-all behavior). Pass raw HTML — do NOT wrap in `<![CDATA[...]]>`, do NOT escape as `<` / `>`. BD stores the field value verbatim; any wrapper/escape gets saved as literal text.

h1.description: **H1 Heading** — Supports template tokens. Rendered as the page's main heading. H1 heading — supports template tokens

h2.description: **H2 Heading** — Supports template tokens. H2 heading — supports template tokens

content.description: Main page body — rendered by a Froala rich text editor. **HTML only, no `<style>` or `<script>` tags** (Froala strips them). Also strips `<form>`, `<input>`, `<select>`, `<textarea>`, and `contenteditable` attributes. **Do NOT put CSS or JS here** — route them to the dedicated fields: CSS → `content_css`, JS/scripts → `content_footer_html`, head-only dependencies → `content_head`. For one-off styling, inline `style="..."` attributes on elements work fine. Supports `[widget=Name]` / `[form=Name]` shortcodes and `%%%template_tokens%%%`. Pass raw HTML — do NOT wrap in `<![CDATA[...]]>`, do NOT escape as `<` / `>`. BD stores the value verbatim.

content_css.description: Custom CSS for this page. **Paste raw CSS rules directly — do NOT wrap in `<style>` tags.** Renders in the page `<head>` at load time. **Scope every selector** under a unique page-specific class or ID (e.g. `.my-about-page h2 { ... }`) — unscoped selectors like `body`, `h1`, `p` affect the entire site. Never target reserved platform classes: `.container`, `.froala-table`, `.image-placeholder` — these are BD utilities, not styling targets.

content_footer_html.description: Page-scoped **JavaScript and other scripts** — rendered just before the closing `</body>` tag. Wrap JS in `<script>` tags here (unlike `content`, this field accepts them). jQuery is already loaded globally on BD sites. Best practice: wrap custom JS in an IIFE `(function($){ /* ... */ })(jQuery);` and scope all selectors to a unique page class so JS doesn't leak to other pages. Also the right place for third-party script embeds scoped to this page only (analytics pixels, chat widgets, schema, etc.). **Not for extra body HTML** — `content` is the body. This field is strictly for script / dependency tags.

content_head.description: Page-scoped `<head>` dependencies — rendered inside the `<head>` tag. Use for `<link>` tags (external stylesheets, preconnect hints, canonical overrides), `<meta>` tags beyond what the standard SEO fields cover, verification tags, structured-data `<script type="application/ld+json">`, and third-party `<script>` tags that MUST load in the head (rare — prefer `content_footer_html` for most JS).

content_footer.description:
⚠️ **MISLEADING NAME — this is NOT page footer HTML.** Misnamed relic column; BD repurposed it as the **page-access gate**. Controls who can view the rendered public URL:
- `""` (empty, default) — **Public For Everyone**
- `"members_only"` — **Only Allow Members** (logged-in members only; non-members hit a login/signup wall)
- `"digital_products"` — **Only Allow Digital Products Buyers** (only buyers of digital-product items on the site)

Finer-grained rules (which members, which plans, etc.) are handled by other related fields BD exposes separately; this column is just the top-level gate. Do NOT put HTML in this field — for page body use `content`, for scripts/JS use `content_footer_html`, for below-body HTML use `content` (there's no separate 'extra HTML below body' field).

content_menu.description: Menu section this page belongs to

content_layout.description: **Full Screen Page Width** — layout flag that enables edge-to-edge sections on this page. `0` (default) = content stays inside the site's normal max-width container (the contained layout used for most static pages). `1` = full-screen width, so individual rows/sections inside `content` can break out to the browser edges (useful for full-bleed background colors, hero-style bands, or image backgrounds that need to span the viewport). Only set to `1` if the page's HTML has sections designed to go edge-to-edge; plain text/content pages don't need it.

menu_layout.description:
Sidebar position and width (integer). Only has an effect when the page has a sidebar configured via `form_name` — ignored on pages without a sidebar.
  1 = Left Wide    (sidebar on the left, wide column) — BD default when unspecified
  2 = Right Wide   (sidebar on the right, wide column)
  3 = Left Slim    (sidebar on the left, narrow column)
  4 = Right Slim   (sidebar on the right, narrow column)
Note the ordering is NOT sequential by side — left positions are 1 and 3, right positions are 2 and 4. Despite the field name, this is the SIDEBAR position setting, not a navigation menu layout.
**Default on create for `profile_search_results` pages:** `3` (Left Slim). For regular `content` pages, omit unless user specifies — BD defaults to 1 (Left Wide).

show_form.description: **Apply NoIndex, NoFollow** — 1 = adds `<meta name="robots" content="noindex,nofollow">` to the page. Auto-applied to protected pages. NOT a contact-form toggle (despite the field name — BD repurposed this column).

form_name.description: **SIDEBAR name** for this page — BD's field is misnamed (it controls the sidebar slot, NOT a contact form). Pass the exact sidebar `name` string. Empty string `""` = no sidebar. Five **Master Default Sidebars** are always available on every site (not returned by `listSidebars` — hardcoded in BD core): `Global Website Search`, `Member Profile Page`, `Personal Post Feed`, `Post Search Result`, `Post Single Page`. Custom sidebars are discoverable via `listSidebars` (returns only site-local customs). If a user names a sidebar that's in NEITHER list → ask them to pick from what's available; don't send an unknown name (BD renders no sidebar). When `form_name` is set, `menu_layout` controls position. **Default on create for `profile_search_results` pages:** `Member Profile Page`.

hide_header_links.description: **Hide Main Menu** — 1 = hides the main navigation menu on this page.

hide_header.description: **Hide Header** — 1 = hides the full site header on this page.

hide_footer.description: **Hide Footer** — 1 = hides the site footer on this page.

hide_top_right.description: **Hide Top Header Menu** — 1 = hides the top-right nav cluster (account/login links).

facebook_title.description: **Social Media Title (Open Graph)** — Title shown when the page is shared on Facebook/LinkedIn/etc. Open Graph title for social sharing

facebook_desc.description: **Social Media Description (Open Graph)** — Description shown on social shares. Open Graph description

facebook_image.description: **Social Media Shared Image** — URL/filename of the OG image. BD recommends at least 200×200px. Open Graph image URL

date_updated.description: Last-update timestamp on the page record (drives the admin-UI "Last Update" display). Format: `YYYYMMDDHHmmss` (14-digit, no separators — e.g. `20260214232533`). **Required on every `createWebPage` and `updateWebPage` call** — always set to the CURRENT timestamp at call time (unless backfilling historical data from a migration, in which case pass the historical value). BD does NOT auto-populate this field; if you omit it, the admin UI shows a blank/stale date.

updated_by.description: Label shown in admin-UI "Updated By" column on the page record. Free-form string (e.g. `"API"`, `"AI Agent"`, an admin username). Recommended on create/update for audit traceability so admins can tell which edits came from the API vs. the UI.

custom_html_placement.description:
Where to render the page's custom SEO content (`content` HTML) in relation to the dynamic search results, on `seo_type=profile_search_results` pages. Only meaningful for search-result static pages (and later `seo_type=data_category`) — ignored on regular `content` pages.
  0 = Inside Tab                (content + member results render in separate nav tabs)
  1 = Above Member Results      (within the results container; respects sidebar width)
  2 = Below Member Results      (within the results container)
  3 = Above Body Content        (full page container width — spans above sidebar + results)
  4 = Below Body Content        (full page container width — below sidebar + results) ← **recommended default for AI-generated SEO pages**
For boilerplate SEO intro paragraphs / FAQ / local copy aimed at bolstering thin pages, `4` is the safest default because it renders full-width below the live results without disrupting the primary member-facing UX.

put.description:
Update an existing web page by `seo_id`. Fields omitted are untouched. Writes live data.

Changes go live immediately. No cache refresh needed for most edits. If changing `filename` (URL slug), consider creating a 301 via `createRedirect` so inbound links to the old slug keep working.

**Use when:** editing copy, meta tags, social-share images, or toggling layout flags (hide header/footer, full-width, etc.). Changing `filename` breaks inbound links — create a `Redirect` via `createRedirect` to preserve SEO.

**Required:** `seo_id`, `date_updated` (YYYYMMDDHHmmss — BD does not auto-populate; blank leaves admin UI "Last Update" stale).

**Common edits:**
- Copy/content: `content`, `h1`, `h2`, `seo_text`, `content_footer`
- SEO: `title`, `meta_desc`, `meta_keywords`
- Social: `facebook_title`, `facebook_desc`, `facebook_image`
- Layout: `content_layout`, `hide_header`, `hide_footer`, etc. (see mapping below)
- Visibility: `content_active` (1 = live, 0 = hidden)

**See also:** `createWebPage` (add new), `deleteWebPage` (remove), `createRedirect` (after slug changes).

**Writes live data:** immediately visible.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...updatedRecord} }`.


**BD Admin UI — field label mapping:**

Some field names are opaque; this is what they're called in the BD admin page-settings screens.

*Display/layout toggles (integer 0/1 unless noted):*
| Field | Admin UI label |
|---|---|
| `content_layout` | Full Screen Page Width |
| `section` | Hide Banner Ad Modules |
| `hide_header` | Hide Header |
| `hide_footer` | Hide Footer |
| `hide_top_right` | Hide Top Header Menu |
| `hide_header_links` | Hide Main Menu |
| `content_images` | Click-Enlarge Images (value: `click_to_enlarge` to enable) |
| `show_form` | **Apply NoIndex, NoFollow** (NOT a contact-form toggle — BD repurposed this column) |
| `disable_css_stylesheets` | Disable Default CSS Stylesheets |
| `disable_preview_screenshot` | Disable Screenshot Preview |
| `seo_text` | Wildcard URL Rewrite (catch-all routing) |

*SEO / meta:*
| Field | Admin UI label |
|---|---|
| `title` | Page Meta Title |
| `meta_desc` | Meta Description |
| `meta_keywords` | Meta Keywords |
| `h1` | H1 Heading |
| `h2` | H2 Heading |

*Social (Open Graph):*
| Field | Admin UI label |
|---|---|
| `facebook_title` | Social Media Title |
| `facebook_desc` | Social Media Description |
| `facebook_image` | Social Media Shared Image (≥200×200px) |

Template tokens: `title`, `meta_desc`, `meta_keywords`, `h1`, `h2` all support `%%%website_name%%%`, `%industry%`, `%profession%` (expanded at render time).

**Hero section (optional banner rendered above page content):** toggled via `enable_hero_section` (`0`=off, `1`=all devices, `2`=hide on mobile). The full hero config spans ~23 fields: `hero_image` (external URLs supported natively — no `auto_image_import` needed), `hero_background_image_size`, `hero_content_overlay_color` (RGB format ONLY, e.g. `rgb(0,0,0)` — hex not accepted), `hero_content_overlay_opacity`, `hero_top_padding`/`hero_bottom_padding`, `hero_column_width`, `hero_alignment`, H1 styling (`h1_font_color`/`h1_font_size`/`h1_font_weight`), H2 styling (`h2_font_color`/`h2_font_size`/`h2_font_weight`), additional content styling (`hero_content_font_color`/`hero_content_font_size`/`hero_section_content`), CTA (`hero_link_url`/`hero_link_text`/`hero_link_target_blank`/`hero_link_size`/`hero_link_color`), and `hero_hide_banner_ad` to suppress the site-wide Below Header Banner Ad on this page. All color fields require RGB format. `enable_hero_section=0` is a master kill-switch — other hero_* values remain stored but are not rendered. The H1/H2 TEXT itself comes from the page's top-level `h1` and `h2` fields; the `h1_*`/`h2_*` here control ONLY hero styling. **Homepage:** the homepage record (`seo_type=home`, typically `seo_id=1`) cannot be CREATED via API — it's system-seeded. For homepage edits use `updateWebPage` with the existing `seo_id`. **Homepage hero fields are BENIGN — stored but not rendered by the homepage template.** BD accepts and persists `enable_hero_section` + any `hero_*`/`h1_*`/`h2_*` values on the homepage record (live-verified 2026-04-19: `seo_id=1` stored `enable_hero_section=1` + `hero_image` + `hero_section_content` cleanly), but the homepage template does not render a hero banner — homepage layout is driven by its own widget/section configuration, not list_seo's hero columns. Writing hero fields to the homepage does no harm (no errors, no visible effect) but also does no good. **Recommendation:** skip hero fields when updating `seo_id=1` unless the user explicitly requests it for data-hygiene reasons. To display a hero on the homepage, use the homepage widget/section configuration instead (not scoped by this API).

**Hero safe-defaults — ALWAYS apply when `enable_hero_section=1` or `2` and the agent is the one enabling the hero** (unless the user explicitly overrides a value). These defaults ensure text is readable over any background image:
- `h1_font_color="rgb(255,255,255)"` — white H1 readable over most hero images
- `h2_font_color="rgb(255,255,255)"` — matching white H2
- `hero_content_overlay_color="rgb(0,0,0)"` — black overlay layer
- `hero_content_overlay_opacity="0.5"` — at 0.5 (admin-UI level 5 of 10), dims the background enough for white text to pop without obscuring the image. **EAV-STORED — `createWebPage` writes this correctly, but `updateWebPage` silently drops it. On updates, write it via `updateUserMeta`/`createUserMeta` with `database=list_seo`. See the EAV workaround section below.**
- `hero_top_padding="100"` — 100px breathing room above hero content
- `hero_bottom_padding="100"` — 100px below

Applies to BOTH `seo_type=content` and `seo_type=profile_search_results` pages. Universal readability rule.

**Cache refresh REQUIRED after any hero create/update:** Hero changes do NOT reflect on the public URL until the site cache is refreshed. After ANY `createWebPage` or `updateWebPage` that touches hero fields (`enable_hero_section`, any `hero_*`, `h1_font_*`, `h2_font_*`), call `refreshSiteCache` immediately. Without the refresh, the admin dashboard will show the changes but visitors will see stale hero markup until BD's next scheduled cache cycle.

**users_meta update workaround — CRITICAL for updates.** BD's `list_seo` table mixes direct columns with EAV-stored values in `users_meta`. On CREATE, `createWebPage` writes all fields correctly. On UPDATE, `updateWebPage` ONLY writes the direct columns — these 18 fields get silently ignored by the update path and must be written via `updateUserMeta` / `createUserMeta` instead: `linked_post_category`, `linked_post_type`, `disable_preview_screenshot`, `disable_css_stylesheets`, `hero_content_overlay_opacity`, `hero_link_target_blank`, `hero_background_image_size`, `hero_link_size`, `hero_link_color`, `hero_content_font_size`, `hero_section_content`, `hero_column_width`, `h2_font_weight`, `h1_font_weight`, `h2_font_size`, `h1_font_size`, `hero_link_text`, `hero_link_url`. Workflow per field: (1) `listUserMeta` filter by `database=list_seo`, `database_id=<seo_id>`, `key=<field>` to find the existing `meta_id`; (2) exists → `updateUserMeta(meta_id, value=...)`; (3) missing → `createUserMeta(database=list_seo, database_id=<seo_id>, key=<field>, value=<new value>)`. Reads merge automatically via `getWebPage`/`listWebPages` — you only need the workaround for WRITES. On `deleteWebPage`, also delete the corresponding users_meta rows (filter by the deleted `seo_id`) to prevent orphans.

**Hero image sourcing for agents:** never use random-image placeholders (picsum.photos, lorempixel, placekitten) — those change per page load and look broken to real visitors. Pick a content-relevant stock photo from Pexels (free license, no attribution required) via a search term derived from the page topic. Use the LARGE variant URL (Pexels provides `?auto=compress&cs=tinysrgb&w=1800` as a query string on the images hostname) — not `original`, which is often 5000+px and too heavy for a hero banner. If the user supplies their own image URL, use that.

**Member Search Results SEO pages (`seo_type=profile_search_results`) — thin-content remedy workflow:** BD auto-generates dynamic search-result pages for every valid combination of location + category on the site (e.g. `california/beverly-hills/plumbers`). Google often penalizes these when they return only 1–2 members (thin content). The remedy is to create a STATIC `list_seo` record with `seo_type=profile_search_results`, `filename=<the exact URL slug>`, and `content=<unique SEO copy>` (intro paragraph, local context, FAQ, etc.) — that static record then overrides the dynamic rendering and the custom copy renders alongside the live results.

**CRITICAL — `filename` MUST be a real location/category slug BD's dynamic router recognizes.** The `profile_search_results` static page works by OVERRIDING an existing dynamic search URL. If the `filename` does NOT correspond to a valid BD dynamic URL built from actual country/state/city/top_category/sub_category slugs on THIS site, **the public URL will return HTTP 404 even though the `list_seo` record exists with `content_active=1`**. BD has no dynamic page to override, so nothing renders. Arbitrary / made-up / pattern-generator / test slugs like `foo-bar-baz`, `my-cool-page`, or `hero-test-page` WILL NOT render as `profile_search_results` pages — live-verified on 2026-04-19. Every segment of the slug must come from a verified live lookup: 
  - country segment (optional left-parent): `listCountries` → lowercase `country_name` with spaces → hyphens
  - state segment: `listStates` → `state_filename`
  - city segment: `listCities` → `city_filename`
  - top-category segment: `listTopCategories` → `filename`
  - sub-category segment: `listSubCategories` → `filename`
Do NOT construct `profile_search_results` slugs from arbitrary strings. If the user wants a static page at an arbitrary URL, use `seo_type=content` instead (content pages create their own route and render at any `filename`).

*URL slug construction (the `filename` field, NO leading slash):* segments use hierarchy order and any left-parent is droppable to broaden the match. Always concatenate with `/` separators:
  - Fullest: `country/state/city/top_cat/sub_cat`
  - Common: `state/city/top_cat/sub_cat` (when site is single-country)
  - `city/top_cat/sub_cat`, `top_cat/sub_cat`, `city`, `state`, `top_cat`, etc.

*Where each segment comes from:*
  - `country` segment: NOT a stored slug — derive from `list_countries.country_name` (via `listCountries`) by lowercasing and replacing spaces with hyphens (e.g. `"United States"` → `united-states`)
  - `state` segment: `location_states.state_filename` (via `listStates`)
  - `city` segment: `location_cities.city_filename` (via `listCities`)
  - `top_cat` segment: top category's `filename` (via `listTopCategories`)
  - `sub_cat` segment: sub-category's `filename` (via `listSubCategories`)

*Agent workflow for "add SEO to [category] in [location] page":*
  1. Resolve each named input to its slug via the relevant list endpoint (`listCities`, `listStates`, `listTopCategories`, `listSubCategories`) filtering by the full/human name field — exact-match `=` is reliable for these.
  2. For ambiguous requests (e.g. user says "Beverly Hills plumbers" — could be `beverly-hills/plumbers` OR `california/beverly-hills/plumbers`), ask the user which URL variant they want UNLESS they've explicitly requested bulk/pattern-based updates.
  3. Check for an existing `list_seo` record at that slug: `listWebPages` with `property=filename&property_value=<slug>&property_operator==`. If found → `updateWebPage`. If not → `createWebPage` with `seo_type=profile_search_results`, `filename=<slug>`, `content=<SEO HTML>`. **Always run the check-first step — BD does NOT reject duplicate `filename` values. If you skip the check and the page already exists, BD silently creates a second row with its own `seo_id`; both records will exist, and which one wins at render time is undefined. Duplicates orphan silently, no warning.**
  4. On create, **required defaults** for `profile_search_results` pages (unless user explicitly overrides):
     - `seo_type=profile_search_results`
     - `content_active=1`
     - `custom_html_placement=4` (Below Body Content — safest for boilerplate SEO intro without disrupting the live member UI)
     - `form_name="Member Profile Page"` (sidebar — one of the one of the 6 Master Default Sidebars; always available on every site)
     - `menu_layout=3` (Left Slim — sidebar position)
     - `date_updated=<current YYYYMMDDHHmmss>` — set on EVERY create and update (BD does not auto-populate; blank leaves admin UI showing no update date)
     - `updated_by` — optional but recommended, e.g. `"AI Agent"` or `"API"` for audit trail
     - **If user requests a different sidebar and it is NOT in the 6 master defaults OR `listSidebars` customs → ask:** "That sidebar isn't available on this site. OK to use `Member Profile Page` (default) instead, or would you like to pick from [list of available]?"
  5. **MUST auto-generate SEO-optimized meta for the specific location+category combo** — don't leave these blank on a search-results SEO page, that defeats the whole purpose. Agent populates the following from the human names (not slugs), using natural "in [city]" / "[city] [category] near me" / "best [category] in [location]" phrasing, respecting each field's char budget, and VARYING Title vs Social Title so social platforms show different copy than the Google snippet:
     - `title` — Page Meta Title. 50–60 chars ideal, ≤70 hard max. Format: `"[Top Sub-category or Top category] in [City], [State] | [Site Name]"` or similar. BD stores the value verbatim — Google/Bing truncate at ~55–60 chars in the SERP snippet, so keep the important words in the first 55. Pre-truncate client-side if you want precise control.
     - `meta_desc` — Meta Description. 150–160 chars ideal, ≤170 max. 1–2 sentence pitch naming the location and category, with a CTA.
     - `meta_keywords` — comma-separated, ~200 chars. Low SEO weight today but fill anyway: `"[category] [city], [sub-cat] [city], [category] near me, [city] [state] [category]"`.
     - `facebook_title` — Open Graph title. 55–60 chars ideal. Should differ from `title` — more conversational for social shares.
     - `facebook_desc` — Open Graph description. 110–125 chars ideal. Shorter than `meta_desc`, punchier for social feeds.
     - **DO NOT auto-set `facebook_image`** — requires an uploaded asset from the customer.
  6. **h1/h2 double-render trap:** if the hero section is enabled (`enable_hero_section=1` or `2`), the page's `h1` and `h2` text fields render inside the hero banner. If your `content` HTML ALSO contains an `<h1>` or `<h2>`, the page renders BOTH — creating duplicate headings (bad for SEO). Safe rule: if you put `<h1>`/`<h2>` inside `content`, leave the page's `h1`/`h2` fields empty; if you want the hero to show the heading, set `h1`/`h2` on the record and don't repeat it in `content`.
  7. `custom_html_placement` is only meaningful on `profile_search_results` (and later `data_category`) page types — ignored on regular `content` pages.
  8. **Country-only slug caveat (live-verified):** `filename=<country_slug>` (e.g. `united-states` by itself) stores as a `list_seo` record but **does NOT render a public URL** — BD has no standalone country-level dynamic search route, so the slug 404s. The country slug is only useful as a LEFT-PARENT PREFIX to a longer slug (e.g. `united-states/california/los-angeles/plumbers`). Country-alone works fine for `content`-type pages via `filename`; the 404 is specific to `profile_search_results`.

BD handles the dynamic-to-static takeover automatically: once a `list_seo` record exists with a matching slug and `content_active=1`, it overrides the dynamic page for that URL. Millions of dynamic search URLs can exist on a site without any `list_seo` rows — they only enter `list_seo` when an admin/API explicitly converts them to static pages.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

delete.description:
Permanently delete a web page by `seo_id`. Destructive — cannot be undone via API.

**Use when:** permanently removing a page. For "hide without deleting" use `updateWebPage` with `content_active=0` — preserves the record and its SEO history, easily re-publishable.

**Required:** `seo_id`.

**Destructive:** confirm with the user. If inbound links or menus reference the deleted page's URL, consider creating a `createRedirect` BEFORE deleting so those links don't 404. Menus with items pointing at this page also need cleanup.

**⚠️ ORPHAN CLEANUP REQUIRED — BD does NOT cascade-delete users_meta rows.** Each WebPage with a hero or custom layout has up to 18 EAV rows in `users_meta` (`database=list_seo`, `database_id=<seo_id>`). These persist after `deleteWebPage` unless you clean them up. Safe post-delete workflow:
  1. Call `listUserMeta` filtered on `database=list_seo` AND `database_id=<deleted seo_id>`.
  2. **Client-side filter the response** — keep only rows whose `database` field equals `list_seo`. The same `database_id` value may exist in `users_meta` pointing at unrelated parent tables (e.g. a member with `user_id=<seo_id>` in `users_data`), and those rows must NOT be deleted.
  3. For each remaining row, call `deleteUserMeta` with `meta_id=<row.meta_id>`, `database=list_seo`, `database_id=<seo_id>` (all three required as of v6.7.1).

Never loop-delete by `database_id` alone — you will silently destroy unrelated records on other tables.

**See also:** `updateWebPage` with `content_active=0` (hide without deleting — safer for reversible actions).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: "list_seo record was deleted" }`.

_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

post.description:
Clears BD's internal template/theme/widget caches. Useful when recent admin edits to design settings or widgets aren't showing on the public site yet.

**Use when:** the user has just updated a template, theme setting, widget, menu, or page layout and the public site is still serving the old version. Also a safe troubleshooting step if they report a recent admin-edit not appearing after ~1 minute.

**Optional parameters:**
- `scope` — target one cache area only (`data_widgets`, `settings`, `web_pages`, `css`, `menus`, `sidebars`). Faster than a full refresh. Omit to refresh all 6.
- `full=1` — include heavier db_optimization + file_permissions passes in addition to the 6 core areas. Slower; use only when the user reports persistent issues and lighter refreshes didn't help.

**Do NOT use for:**
- New pages — freshly created pages via `createWebPage` are live immediately via `content_active=1` + valid `seo_type`. This endpoint does NOT register new URLs with the public router.
- Routine workflow noise — do not call after every bulk op. Most BD writes are live immediately; cache invalidation is a targeted fallback, not a default post-step.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: "Cache refreshed successfully", areas_refreshed: [...], scope: "full", full: false }`. The `areas_refreshed` array lists exactly what was cleared — useful for logging or reporting back to the user. Example default response:
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "message": "Cache refreshed successfully",
  "areas_refreshed": ["data_widgets", "settings", "web_pages", "css", "menus", "sidebars"],
  "scope": "full",
  "full": false
}
```

With `full=1` the `areas_refreshed` additionally includes `db_optimization` and `file_permissions`. Invalid `scope` values return an error listing the valid set: `{ status: "error", message: "Invalid scope value: <x>. Valid values: ..." }`.

Undocumented by BD publicly; exposed via admin API-permissions UI.

get.description:
Paginated enumeration of TOP-level member categories. Read-only.

Top Categories are the highest level of the 3-tier member classification (e.g., "Restaurants", "Dentists"). Each record's `profession_id` is what populates `users_data.profession_id` on member records. Backed by BD's `list_professions` table.

**Use when:** populating a category dropdown, generating a site map, or discovering the `profession_id` of an existing category before assigning members to it. Returns ALL top-level categories. For sub-categories under a specific top, use `listSubCategories` with a `profession_id` filter.

**Permission note — platform gap:** this endpoint (`/api/v2/list_professions/*`) is NOT in BD's public Swagger spec, so the admin's API key permissions UI does NOT auto-generate a toggle for it. New keys default to DENY on this path even if the admin enables the "Categories (Professions)" toggle — that UI toggle gates the Swagger-documented `/api/v2/category/*` endpoints (a DIFFERENT legacy table). On a 403 here: the fix is to MANUALLY INSERT a row into `bd_api_key_permissions` for `endpoint_path='/api/v2/list_professions/get'` (and the singular `/api/v2/list_professions/get/{profession_id}` for `getTopCategory`). This is a platform-level gap worth reporting to BD dev team. Do NOT substitute `/api/v2/category/*` as a fallback — it reads a different, possibly-empty table and returns inconsistent data.

**Pagination:** cursor-based. Pass `limit` (default 25, max 100) and `page` token from the previous response's `next_page`. Do not assume integer offsets.

**Filter/sort:** `property` + `property_value` + `property_operator` (`=`, `LIKE`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`) to filter; `order_column` + `order_type` (`ASC`/`DESC`) to sort.

**See also:** `getTopCategory` (single by ID), `listSubCategories` (sub-categories filtered by `profession_id`), `createTopCategory` (add new).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", total, current_page, total_pages, next_page, prev_page, message: [...records] }`. Each record has `profession_id`, `name`, `desc`, `filename`, `keywords`, `icon`, `sort_order`, `lead_price`, `image`, `revision_timestamp`.


---

**Member Category Hierarchy (3 levels):**

BD classifies members through a 3-level taxonomy — AI agents MUST understand all three to correctly create, assign, and query member categories:

| Level | Tool nicknames | Endpoint | BD internal table | Key field | Parent reference |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1. Top Category** | `listTopCategories`, `createTopCategory`, etc. | `/api/v2/list_professions/*` | `list_professions` | `profession_id` (PK) | — |
| **2. Sub Category** | `listSubCategories`, `createSubCategory`, etc. | `/api/v2/list_services/*` | `list_services` | `service_id` (PK) | `profession_id` → parent Top Category; `master_id` → parent Sub Category (for sub-sub nesting; 0 = direct child of Top) |
| **3. Member ↔ Sub Category link** | `listMemberSubCategoryLinks`, `createMemberSubCategoryLink`, etc. | `/api/v2/rel_services/*` | `rel_services` | `rel_id` (PK) | `user_id` → member; `service_id` → Sub Category |

**How a member gets classified on their public profile:**
- `users_data.profession_id` → points at a single Top Category (the member's primary classification; shown in URL slug)
- `users_data.services` → CSV of Sub Category IDs the member is tagged with (multiple allowed; simpler than the join table)
- `rel_services` rows (Member ↔ Sub Category links) → used when you need per-link metadata like `avg_price`, `specialty`, `num_completed`. Optional; most sites use just the CSV field.

**Sub-sub-categories:** `createSubCategory` with `master_id=<parent service_id>` creates a Sub Category nested under another Sub Category (a "sub-sub"). `master_id=0` (default) means the Sub Category sits directly under a Top Category (the `profession_id`).

**There is NO `createProfession` or `createService` tool in this MCP** — those are the BD internal resource names. Use the tools named above.


_Auth: `X-Api-Key` header. Rate limit: 100 req/60s (on 429, back off 60s). Errors: `{ "status": "error", "message": "..." }` with standard HTTP codes._

post.description:
Create a new TOP-level member category. Writes live data.

A Top Category is the highest level of the 3-tier member classification (e.g., "Restaurants"). It populates the `profession_id` field on user records. Backed by BD's `list_professions` table.

**Use when:** explicitly building out the site's taxonomy BEFORE members exist. If you're creating members and want categories to be auto-created by name, skip this and pass `profession_name` directly to `createUser` — BD auto-creates missing top-level categories on member-create.

**Required:** `name`, `filename`.

**Parameter guidance:**
- `name` — human-readable (e.g. "Restaurants", "Dentists")
- `filename` — URL-slug form (e.g. "restaurants") used in public member profile URLs
- `desc`, `keywords`, `icon`, `sort_order`, `lead_price`, `image` — all optional

**See also:** `listTopCategories` (list all), `getTopCategory` (by ID), `createSubCategory` (add a sub-category under this top).

**Writes live data:** changes are immediately visible on the public site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...createdRecord} }` including the new `profession_id`. Use that value to populate `users_data.profession_id` on member records.

**Common workflow — full 3-tier setup ("create Restaurants → Sushi → assign Alice"):**

1. `createTopCategory` with `name="Restaurants"`, `filename="restaurants"` → returns `profession_id` (e.g. 42)
2. `createSubCategory` with `name="Sushi"`, `profession_id=42`, `filename="sushi"` → returns `service_id` (e.g. 17)
3. Assign Alice: `updateUser` with `user_id=<Alice>`, `profession_id=42`, `services="17"` (simple), OR `createMemberSubCategoryLink` with `user_id=<Alice>`, `service_id=17`, `avg_price=...`, `specialty=1` (with per-link metadata)

put.description:
Update an existing TOP-level member category by `profession_id`. Fields omitted are untouched. Writes live data.

**Use when:** renaming a category, changing its URL slug (`filename`), updating SEO keywords, or reordering. Changing `filename` breaks inbound links — also create a `Redirect` via `createRedirect` to preserve SEO.

**Required:** `profession_id`.

**See also:** `createTopCategory` (add new), `deleteTopCategory` (remove).

**Writes live data:** changes are immediately visible on the public site.

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: {...updatedRecord} }`.

profession_id.description: The top-level category ID to delete.

delete.description:
Permanently delete a TOP-level member category by `profession_id`. Destructive — cannot be undone via API.

**Use when:** removing an unused top-level category. Any members with matching `profession_id` become orphaned — reassign them first. Any Sub Categories (`list_services` rows) under this top also orphan — delete or re-parent them.

**Required:** `profession_id`.

**Destructive:** confirm intent with the user. Members who referenced this `profession_id` will have orphan references. Sub Categories under this top (with matching `profession_id` in `list_services`) also become orphaned — consider reassigning or deleting them first.

**See also:** `updateTopCategory` (modify without removing).

**Returns:** `{ status: "success", message: "list_professions record was deleted" }`.
